en: It is not easy to talk about the role of the mass media in this overwhelmingly significant phase in European history. (20 words)
cn: 讨论大众传媒在欧洲史上这个极其重要的阶段所起的作用不是一件容易的事。

en: History and news become confused and one's impressions tend to be a mixture of skepticism and optimism. (18 words)
cn: 历史与新闻常常混为一谈，人们的印象往往是怀疑与乐观的混合。

en: (46) Television is one of the means by which these feelings are created and conveyed—and perhaps never before has it served so much to connect different peoples and nations as in the recent events in Europe. (36 words)
cn: （46）电视是引发和传递这些感受的手段之一；在欧洲近来发生的事情中，它把不同的民族和国家连到一起，其作用之大，前所未有。

en: The Europe that is now forming cannot be anything other than its peoples, their cultures and national identities. (18 words)
cn: 欧洲现在的格局正是由多个民族、多种文化以及不同的民族特点组成的。

en: With this in mind we can begin to analyze the European television scene. (13 words)
cn: 有了这样一种认识，我们就可以开始对欧洲的电视界做一些分析了。

en: (47) In Europe, as elsewhere, multi-media groups have been increasingly successful: groups which bring together television, radio, newspapers, magazines and publishing houses that work in relation to one another. (28 words)
cn: （47）在欧洲，像在其他地方一样，传媒集团越来越成功：这些集团将相关的电视、广播、报纸、杂志和出版社组合在一起。

en: One Italian example would be the Berlusconi group while abroad Maxwell and Murdoch come to mind. (17 words)
cn: 比方说意大利的贝鲁斯科尼集团；而在国外，则让人想到马克斯韦尔和默多克。

en: Clearly, only the biggest and most flexible television companies are going to be able to compete in such a rich and hotly-contested market. (23 words)
cn: 很明显，在这样一个丰富多彩、竞争激烈的市场上，只有那些规模最大、灵活性最强的电视公司才有可能具有竞争力。

en: (48) This alone demonstrates that the television business is not an easy world to survive in, a fact underlined by statistics that show that out of eighty European television networks, no less than 50% took a loss in 1989. (38 words)
cn: （48）这一点就足以表明，要在电视行业里生存下来并非易事。统计数字尤其说明了这一事实，在80个欧洲电视网中，多达一半在1989年亏损。

en: Moreover, the integration of the European community will oblige television companies to cooperate more closely in terms of both production and distribution. (21 words)
cn: 此外，欧共体的一体化将迫使电视公司在制作和发行方面进行更密切的合作。

en: (49) Creating a "European identity" that respects the different cultures and traditions which go to make up the connecting fabric of the Old Continent is no easy task and demands a strategic choice—that of producing programs in Europe for Europe. (39 words)
cn: （49）不同的文化和传统把欧洲大陆编织在一起，要创造出一种尊重这些不同文化和传统的"欧洲品牌"绝非易事，需要人们做出战略性的选择——即在欧洲制作出为欧洲服务的节目。

en: This entails reducing our dependence on the North American market, whose programs relate to experiences and cultural traditions which are different from our own. (24 words)
cn: 这就要求我们减少对北美市场的依赖，因为他们的节目涉及的经历和文化传统与我们的大不相同。

en: In order to achieve these objectives, we must concentrate more on co-productions, the exchange of news, documentary services and training. (19 words)
cn: 要想达到这些目的，我们必须更加注重联合制作、新闻交换、文献服务和培训。

en: This also involves the agreements between European countries for the creation of a European bank for Television Production which, on the model of the European Investments Bank, will handle the finances necessary for production costs. (34 words)
cn: 同时还要求欧洲各国就建立欧洲电视制作银行达成一致意见，这个电视制作银行将以欧洲投资银行为榜样，为电视制作解决必要的资金问题。

en: (50) In dealing with a challenge on such a scale, it is no exaggeration to say "United we stand, divided we fall"—and if I had to choose slogan it would be "Unity in our diversity," a unity of objectives that nonetheless respect the varied peculiarities of each country. (48 words)
cn: （50）在应对如此规模的挑战时，可以毫不夸张地说："如果团结，我们就成功；如果分裂，我们就失败"——如果我必须选一句口号，那就是"同中存异"：目标一致，但同时尊重每个国家各自的特性。

en: Is it true that the American intellectual is rejected and considered of no account in his society? (17 words)
cn: 美国知识分子真的受到排斥，并且被认为对社会没有一点价值了吗？

en: I am going to suggest that it is not true. (10 words)
cn: 我要告诉大家的是，事实并非如此。

en: Father Bruckberger told part of the story when he observed that it is the intellectuals who have rejected Americans. (19 words)
cn: 布鲁克伯杰神父曾一语道破部分真相，他认为是知识分子抛弃了美国。

en: But they have done more than that. (7 words)
cn: 但是知识分子的所作所为更为过火。

en: They have grown dissatisfied with the role of the intellectual. (10 words)
cn: 他们已经对自己作为知识分子的角色变得深感不满。

en: It is they, not Americans, who have become anti-intellectual. (9 words)
cn: 反对知识分子的是他们自己，而不是美国。

en: First, the object of our study pleads for definition. (9 words)
cn: 首先，我们需要对我们的研究对象有一个界定。

en: What is an intellectual? (4 words)
cn: 什么是知识分子？

en: (46) I shall define him as an individual who has elected as his primary duty and pleasure in life the activity of thinking in a Socratic way about moral problems. (30 words)
cn: （46）我会把知识分子定义为这样的人：他把以苏格拉底的方式来思考道德问题作为人生中的首要任务和乐趣。

en: He explores such problems consciously, articulately, and frankly, first by asking factual questions, then by asking moral questions, finally by suggesting action which seems appropriate in light of the factual and moral information which he has obtained. (36 words)
cn: 他以自觉、清晰以及坦诚的方式来探讨这些问题，首先从事实的角度提出问题，再从道德的角度提出问题，最后，根据他所获得的事实材料和道德材料，提出适当的解决办法。

en: (47) His function is analogous to that of a judge, who must accept the obligation of revealing in as obvious a manner as possible the course of reasoning which led him to his decision. (33 words)
cn: （47）他的职责与法官相似，必须承担这样的责任：用尽可能清楚明白的方式来展示自己做出决定的推理过程。

en: This definition excludes many individuals usually referred to as intellectuals—the average scientist, for one. (15 words)
cn: 以这个定义来衡量，有很多通常所谓的知识分子，其实并不能算作真正的知识分子——普通科学家就是其中之一。

en: (48) I have excluded him because, while his accomplishments may contribute to the solution of moral problems, he has not been charged with the task of approaching any but the factual aspects of those problems. (34 words)
cn: （48）我之所以将普通科学家排除在外，是因为尽管他的成果可能有助于解决道德问题，但他承担的任务只不过是研究这些问题的事实方面。

en: Like other human beings, he encounters moral issues even in the every-day performance of his routine duties—he is not supposed to cook his experiments, manufacture evidence, or doctor his reports. (30 words)
cn: 和其他人一样，即使在履行其日常职责的时候，普通科学家也面临种种道德问题——他不应该篡改实验、炮制证据或者伪造报告。

en: (49) But his primary task is not to think about the moral code, which governs his activity, any more than a businessman is expected to dedicate his energies to an exploration of rules of conduct in business. (37 words)
cn: （49）但是他（普通科学家）的首要任务并不是考虑支配自己行动的道德规范，正如我们并不指望商人专注于探索商业行为准则一样。

en: During most of his waking life he will take his code for granted, as the businessman takes his ethics. (19 words)
cn: 如同生意人将其职业道德视为理所当然一样，科学家在其清醒的大部分时间里也将其职业准则视为理所当然。

en: The definition also excludes the majority of teachers, despite the fact that teaching has traditionally been the method whereby many intellectuals earn their living. (23 words)
cn: 该定义也不包括大部分教师，尽管存在这样的事实：传统上，教学一直是许多知识分子谋生的手段。

en: (50) They may teach very well, and more than earn their salaries, but most of them make little or no independent reflections on human problems which involve moral judgment. (28 words)
cn: （50）他们（很多老师）可能擅长教书，而且不仅仅是为了挣钱。但是他们大多数人对涉及道德判断的人类问题很少或者根本没有进行独立思考。

en: This description even fits the majority of eminent scholars. (9 words)
cn: 这样的说法甚至也适用于大部分杰出的学者。

en: Being learned in some branch of human knowledge is one thing; living in "public and illustrious thoughts," as Emerson would say, is something else. (25 words)
cn: 在某个知识领域博学是一回事；在生活中"能为公众着想并且勤于思考"，正如爱默生所言，又是另外一回事。

en: The study of law has been recognized for centuries as a basic intellectual discipline in European universities. (18 words)
cn: 几个世纪以来，欧洲的各所大学一直认为法学是一门基本知识学科。

en: However, only in recent years has it become a feature of undergraduate programs in Canadian universities. (17 words)
cn: 然而，只是在最近几年，法学才成为加拿大大学本科专业的一个特色。

en: (46) Traditionally, legal learning has been viewed in such institutions as the special preserve of lawyers, rather than a necessary part of the intellectual equipment of an educated person. (28 words)
cn: （46）传统上，这些学校一直把法律学习看作是律师们所专有的，而不是一个受过教育的人知识储备的必要组成部分。

en: Happily, the older and more continental view of legal education is establishing itself in a number of Canadian universities and some have even begun to offer undergraduate degrees in law. (30 words)
cn: 可喜的是，加拿大的许多大学正在树立更传统、更具欧洲大陆特色的法律教育观点，有些大学甚至已经开始授予法学学士学位。

en: If the study of law is beginning to establish itself as part and parcel of a general education, its aims and methods should appeal directly to journalism educators. (28 words)
cn: 如果法学正在开始成为普通教育的重要组成部分的话，那么它的目标和方法应该会立刻吸引新闻学教育者。

en: Law is a discipline which encourages responsible judgment. (8 words)
cn: 法律是一门鼓励进行责任判断的学科。

en: On the one hand, it provides opportunities to analyze such ideas as justice, democracy and freedom. (17 words)
cn: 一方面，它为分析像正义、民主和自由这样的概念提供机会。

en: (47) On the other, it links these concepts to everyday realities in a manner which is parallel to the links journalists forge on a daily basis as they cover and comment on the news. (33 words)
cn: （47）另一方面，法律以一种方式把这些观念和日常生活联系起来——这与新闻记者每天做新闻报道和新闻评论的做法相同。

en: For example, notions of evidence and fact, of basic rights and public interest are at work in the process of journalistic judgment and production just as in courts of law. (29 words)
cn: 比如，有关证据和事实、基本权利和公共利益这样的概念在新闻判断和新闻编写过程中就如同在法庭上一样发挥作用。

en: Sharpening judgment by absorbing and reflecting on law is a desirable component of a journalist's intellectual preparation for his or her career. (21 words)
cn: 通过研读并思考法律来提高判断能力是一名新闻记者为其事业应该做的一项知识准备。

en: (48) But the idea that the journalist must understand the law more profoundly than an ordinary citizen rests on an understanding of the established conventions and special responsibilities of the news media. (30 words)
cn: （48）但是"记者必须比普通市民更加深刻地了解法律"这种观点取决于对新闻媒体的既定习俗和特殊责任的了解。

en: Politics or, more broadly, the functioning of the state, is a major subject for journalists. (16 words)
cn: 政治，或者广义上说，国家的职能，是新闻记者报道的一个主要方面。

en: The better informed they are about the way the state works, the better their reporting will be. (18 words)
cn: 他们对国家的机制了解得越多，他们的报道就越优秀。

en: (49) In fact, it is difficult to see how journalists who do not have a clear grasp of the basic features of the Canadian Constitution can do a competent job on political stories. (33 words)
cn: （49）事实上，要明白那些还没有清晰地领会加拿大宪法基本特征的记者如何能够胜任政治新闻的报道工作非常困难。

en: Furthermore, the legal system and the events which occur within it are primary subjects for journalists. (16 words)
cn: 此外，法律体系及其中发生的事件是新闻记者报道的主题。

en: While the quality of legal journalism varies greatly, there is an undue reliance amongst many journalists on interpretations supplied to them by lawyers. (23 words)
cn: 虽然法律报道的质量各不相同，但是许多新闻记者都过分依赖律师提供给他们的诠释。

en: (50) While comment and reaction from lawyers may enhance stories, it is preferable for journalists to rely on their own notions of significance and make their own judgments. (27 words)
cn: （50）虽然律师所提供的评论和反馈可能会提高新闻报道的质量，但是记者要依靠自己的价值观自己作出判断则更加难能可贵。

en: These can only come from a well-grounded understanding of the legal system. (13 words)
cn: 这些判断只能来自对法律体系的充分理解

en: In his autobiography, Darwin himself speaks of his intellectual powers with extraordinary modesty. (13 words)
cn: 达尔文在其自传中用极其谦逊的口气评价了自己的智力。

en: He points out that he always experienced much difficulty in expressing himself clearly and concisely, but (16 words)
cn: 他指出，想要简洁明了地表达自己观点的时候，他总会遇到很大的困难。

en: (46) he believes that this very difficulty may have had the compensating advantage of forcing him to think long and intently about every sentence, and thus enabling him to detect errors in reasoning and in his own observations. (35 words)
cn: （46）他相信，正是这种困难或许能够扬长补短，迫使他长时间专注地思考每一句话，从而，使他在推理和观察中发现自己的错误。

en: He disclaimed the possession of any great quickness of apprehension or wit, such as distinguished Huxley. (16 words)
cn: 他认为自己完全不具备杰出的赫胥黎那样的反应速度和理解力。

en: (47) He asserted, also, that his power to follow a long and purely abstract train of thought was very limited, for which reason he felt certain that he never could have succeeded with mathematics. (33 words)
cn: （47）他还坚持认为自己进行长时间完全抽象思维的能力十分有限，由此他认定自己在数学方面根本不可能有大的作为。

en: His memory, too, he described as extensive, but hazy. (10 words)
cn: 他还认为自己的记忆杂乱而模糊。

en: So poor in one sense was it that he never could remember for more than a few days a single date or a line of poetry. (25 words)
cn: 从某一个方面来说，甚至是很糟糕，以至于他记住某个日子或者是一行诗歌的话，几天就会忘记。

en: (48) On the other hand, he did not accept as well founded the charge made by some of his critics that, while he was a good observer, he had no power of reasoning. (31 words)
cn: （48）另一方面，一些批评家指责他虽然善于观察，却不具备推理能力，而他认为这种说法缺乏依据。

en: This, he thought, could not be true, because the "Origin of Species" is one long argument from the beginning to the end, and has convinced many able men. (27 words)
cn: 他认为，这种批评是错误的，因为《物种起源》这本书从头到尾都是长篇大论，却说服了很多有才华的人。

en: No one, he submits, could have written it without possessing some power of reasoning. (15 words)
cn: 他承认，如果不具备推理能力，没有人能够写出这样的书。

en: He was willing to assert that "I have a fair share of invention, and of common sense or judgment, such as every fairly successful lawyer or doctor must have, but not, I believe, in any higher degree." (37 words)
cn: 他愿意这样说自己："我具有一定的发现能力和常识判断能力，正如每一个成功的律师和医生必须具备的一样；但是，我认为，我的水平不高。"

en: (49) He adds humbly that perhaps he was "superior to the common run of men in noticing things which easily escape attention, and in observing them carefully." (27 words)
cn: （49）他谦虚地补充道，或许与常人相比，他更能注意到容易忽略的事情，更能对此加以仔细观察。

en: Writing in the last year of his life, he expressed the opinion that in two or three respects his mind had changed during the preceding twenty or thirty years. (29 words)
cn: 在生命最后一年的写作中，他表示，在过去的二三十年中，他的思想在两三个方面发生了变化。

en: Up to the age of thirty or beyond it poetry of many kinds gave him great pleasure. (18 words)
cn: 到三十多岁的时候，诗歌带给他极大的快乐。

en: Formerly, too, pictures had given him considerable, and music very great, delight. (13 words)
cn: 以前，绘画也能给他带来兴致，而音乐能给他无穷的乐趣。

en: In 1881, however, he said, "Now for many years I cannot endure to read a line of poetry. (19 words)
cn: 然而，他在1881年说："这几年来，读一行诗，我就受不了。

en: I have also almost lost my taste for pictures or music." (12 words)
cn: 我也几乎快要丧失了自己对音乐和绘画的品位了。"

en: (50) Darwin was convinced that the loss of these tastes was not only a loss of happiness, but might possibly be injurious to the intellect and more probably to the moral character. (32 words)
cn: （50）达尔文确信，没有了这些爱好不只是少了乐趣，而且可能有损于一个人的思维能力，甚至可能导致一个人道德品质的下降。

en: There is a marked difference between the education which every one gets from living with others and the deliberate educating of the young. (24 words)
cn: 每个人在与别人共同生活中所受到的教育和有意识地对年轻人进行的教育，这两者有明显的区别。

en: In the former case the education is incidental; it is natural and important, but it is not the express reason of the association. (23 words)
cn: 就前者而言，教育是偶然的；这种教育是自然的、重要的，但它并不是人类联合的确切的原因。

en: (46) It may be said that the measure of the worth of any social institution is its effect in enlarging and improving experience, but this effect is not a part of its original motive. (32 words)
cn: （46）我们可以说，衡量任何社会机构价值的标准，是看它在丰富和完善人生经验过程中的影响，但是这种影响并不是它原来就想要达到的。

en: Religious associations began, for example, in the desire to secure the favor of overruling powers and to ward off evil influences; family life in the desire to gratify appetites and secure family perpetuity; systematic labor, for the most part, because of enslavement to others, etc. (42 words)
cn: 例如宗教的联合始于希望取得统治力量的恩赐和避开罪恶的影响；家庭生活始于希望满足各种欲望和使家庭永存；系统性的劳动，主要是为了奴役别人等。

en: (47) Only gradually was the by-product of the institution noted, and only more gradually still was this effect considered as a directive factor in the conduct of the institution. (28 words)
cn: （47）机构附带产生的影响只有逐渐地才能被注意到；而这种影响被视为机构运作的指导性因素的过程则更加缓慢。

en: Even today, in our industrial life, apart from certain values of industriousness and thrift, the intellectual and emotional reaction of the forms of human association under which the world's work is carried on receives little attention as compared with physical output. (41 words)
cn: 甚至在今天，在我们的工业社会中，除了某些勤奋和节俭的价值观念以外，世间工作得以进行的人类联合的各种形式的理智的和情感的反应，和物质产品比较起来，所受到的关注微乎其微。

en: But in dealing with the young, the fact of association itself as an immediate human fact, gains in importance. (20 words)
cn: 但是对待年轻人，人类联合的事实本身，作为当前的人类事实，具有极其的重要性。

en: (48) While it is easy to ignore in our contact with them the effect of our acts upon their disposition, it is not so easy as in dealing with adults. (29 words)
cn: （48）虽然在与年轻人交往的时候，我们很容易忽视我们的行为对他们性格的影响；但是在与成年人打交道时更容易忽略这种影响。

en: The need of training is too evident; the pressure to accomplish a change in their attitude and habits is too urgent to leave these consequences wholly out of account. (28 words)
cn: 训练的需要太明显了，改变他们的态度和习惯的要求很急迫，以致不能对这些后果完全不加以考虑。

en: (49) Since our chief business with them is to enable them to share in a common life we cannot help considering whether or not we are forming the powers which will secure this ability. (33 words)
cn: （49）由于我们对待年轻人的主要任务是使他们能够参与共同生活，因此我们禁不住要考虑，我们是否正在培养一种能确保他们获得这种能力的力量。

en: If humanity has made some headway in realizing that the ultimate value of every institution is its distinctively human effect we may well believe that this lesson has been learned largely through dealings with the young. (34 words)
cn: 如果人类在认识各种制度的最终价值在于它对人生的特殊影响这一点上已经取得一定进展，我们可以深信，这个启示主要是通过和年轻人相处学到的。

en: (50) We are thus led to distinguish, within the broad educational process which we have been so far considering, a more formal kind of education—that of direct tuition or schooling. (29 words)
cn: （50）因此，在目前为止我们一直在讨论的更为宽泛的教育过程中，可以使我们区别出一种更正规的教育——直接讲授或学校教育。

en: In undeveloped social groups, we find very little formal teaching and training. (12 words)
cn: 在不发达的社会群体中，很少有正规的教学和训练。

en: These groups mainly rely for instilling needed dispositions into the young upon the same sort of association which keeps adults loyal to their group. (24 words)
cn: 这些群体为把必需的性格灌输给年轻人，主要依靠使成年人忠于他们群体的相同的联合。

en: One basic weakness in a conservation system based wholly on economic motives is that most members of the land community have no economic value. (24 words)
cn: 在一个完全以经济动机为基础的保护系统中，一个根本的弱点是，陆地生物群落的大部分物种没有经济价值。

en: Yet these creatures are members of the biotic community and, if its stability depends on its integrity, they are entitled to continuance. (22 words)
cn: 但它们是生物群落的一部分，而且，如果生物群落的稳定性取决于其整体性，那么这些生物也有延续生存的资格。

en: When one of these noneconomic categories is threatened and, if we happen to love it, we invent excuses to give it economic importance. (23 words)
cn: 如果某种无经济价值的物种受到威胁，并且碰巧我们喜欢它，我们就会找借口使它具有经济价值。

en: At the beginning of century songbirds were supposed to be disappearing. (11 words)
cn: 在20世纪初，一些鸣禽就快消失的时候，

en: (46) Scientists jumped to the rescue with some distinctly shaky evidence to the effect that insects would eat us up if birds failed to control them. (27 words)
cn: （46）科学家们迅速赶来挽救这种局面，他们使用的证据明显站不住脚，大意是说：如果鸟不能控制这些昆虫，昆虫就会把一切都吃光。

en: The evidence had to be economic in order to be valid. (11 words)
cn: 为了达到效果，这个证据必须和经济相关。

en: It is painful to read these round about accounts today. (11 words)
cn: 现在读到这些绕弯子的说法让人非常痛苦。

en: We have no land ethic yet, (5 words)
cn: 我们还没有土地伦理的意识，

en: (47) but we have at least drawn nearer the point of admitting that birds should continue as a matter of intrinsic right, regardless of the presence or absence of economic advantage to us. (32 words)
cn: （47）但是，我们至少近乎承认，无论鸟类能否给我们带来经济价值，它们自有其生存下去的权利。

en: A parallel situation exists in respect of predatory mammals and fish-eating birds. (12 words)
cn: 同样的情况也出现在肉食性哺乳动物和食鱼的鸟类身上。

en: (48) Time was when biologists somewhat overworked the evidence that these creatures preserve the health of game by killing the physically weak, or that they prey only on "worthless species". (29 words)
cn: （48）有一种证据认为，这些生物通过猎杀弱者来保持猎物的健康状态，或者说，这些生物捕杀的只不过是"没有价值的物种"；曾有段时间，生物学家有点滥用这种证据。

en: Some species of tree have been read out of the party by economics-minded foresters because they grow too slowly or have too low a sale value to pay as timber crops. (31 words)
cn: 一些有经济头脑的森林管理员已经不再把某些树种看成是森林的一部分了，因为这些树木生长得太缓慢，或者几乎丧失了作为木材的销售价值。

en: (49) In Europe, where forestry is ecologically more advanced, the non-commercial tree species are recognized as members of the native forest community, to be preserved as such, within reason. (28 words)
cn: （49）在欧洲，其林业在生态上更加发达，公益林被认为是原始森林群落的一部分，得到相应的保护，在合理范围内。

en: To sum up: a system of conservation based solely on economic self-interest is hopelessly lopsided. (16 words)
cn: 总之，一个仅仅建立在经济利己主义基础上的保护体系是严重失衡、无可救药的。

en: (50) It tends to ignore, and thus eventually to eliminate, many elements in the land community that lack commercial value, but that are essential to its healthy functioning. (28 words)
cn: （50）这种保护体系往往会忽视，并因此最终消除陆生群落中的诸多物种。虽然这些物种缺乏商业价值，但是对于其健康运作却至关重要。

en: It assumes, falsely, I think, that the economic parts of the biotic clock will function without the uneconomic parts. (20 words)
cn: 这种系统假定，即使没有那些不经济的部件，这套生物系统依然能够运转。我认为其实不然。

en: With its theme that "Mind is the master weaver," creating our inner character and outer circumstances, the book As a Man Thinketh by James Allen is an in-depth exploration of the central idea of self-help writing. (37 words)
cn: 《思考的人》是一本深入研究自我救助的励志书籍，作者詹姆斯·艾伦在书中试图告诉我们："人的心智是一位编织大师"，它造就了我们的内在性格和外在环境。

en: (46) Allen's contribution was to take an assumption we all share—that because we are not robots we therefore control our thoughts—and reveal its erroneous nature. (26 words)
cn: （46）我们都不是机器人，因此能够控制自己的思想。艾伦的贡献就在于他去研究了这一假想，并揭示了其错误的本质。

en: Because most of us believe that mind is separate from matter, we think that thoughts can be hidden and made powerless; this allows us to think one way and act another. (31 words)
cn: 因为大多数人相信，思维是脱离物质而存在的，所以我们认为，思想能够隐藏，可以变得全无影响力；如此一来，我们便可思行不一。

en: However, Allen believed that the unconscious mind generates as much action as the conscious mind, and (16 words)
cn: 然而，艾伦相信，人的行为受意识与潜意识的双重支配，且它们的作用相当。

en: (47) while we may be able to sustain the illusion of control through the conscious mind alone, in reality we are continually faced with a question: "Why cannot I make myself do this or achieve that?" (34 words)
cn: （47）我们或许仅仅通过有意识的思维活动就可以维持这种能控制自己思想的幻觉，但事实上我们却不断地碰到一个问题："为什么我不能让自己去做这件事情，实现那个目标呢？"

en: Since desire and will are damaged by the presence of thoughts that do not accord with desire, Allen concluded: "We do not attract what we want, but what we are." (29 words)
cn: 由于与欲望相悖的思想破坏了我们的渴求与期冀，因此，艾伦得出结论："我们并不能激发出自己想要的样子，但是能够激发出自己本身所具有的潜能。"

en: Achievement happens because you as a person embody the external achievement; you don't "get" success but become it. (19 words)
cn: 你之所以能够取得成就，是因为作为一个人，你表现出的是你外在的成就；你并非"获得"成功，而是将成功变为现实。

en: There is no gap between mind and matter. (8 words)
cn: 思维与物质之间没有鸿沟。

en: Part of the fame of Allen's book is its contention that "Circumstances do not make a person, they reveal him." (19 words)
cn: 艾伦这本书的出彩之处还在于，书中指出，"环境并不造就人，而是使人得以彰显自我"。

en: (48) This seems a justification for neglect of those in need, and a rationalization of exploitation, of the superiority of those at the top and the inferiority of those at the bottom. (30 words)
cn: （48）这种说法似乎为忽视那些贫困者的行为找到了借口，使剥削合理化，令上层人优越，底层人卑微。

en: This, however, would be a knee-jerk reaction to a subtle argument. (11 words)
cn: 然而，这只不过是对一个精妙论点的一种本能的草率反应。

en: Each set of circumstances, however bad, offers a unique opportunity for growth. (13 words)
cn: 每一种环境，即使恶劣，对于成长来说，都是独一无二的机会。

en: If circumstances always determined the life and prospects of people, then humanity would never have progressed. (17 words)
cn: 如果环境总是决定个人的生活和未来，那么人类将永远不会进步。

en: In fact, (2 words)
cn: 事实上，

en: (49) circumstances seem to be designed to bring out the best in us and if we feel that we have been "wronged" then we are unlikely to begin a conscious effort to escape from our situation. (34 words)
cn: （49）环境似乎旨在激发我们最大的潜能，如果我们总感觉"上天不公"，那么就不太可能会自觉地努力摆脱现状。

en: Nevertheless, as any biographer knows, a person's early life and its conditions are often the greatest gift to an individual. (20 words)
cn: 但是，如同任何一位传记作家所知的那样，一个人的早期生活以及当时的际遇常常是他人生最大的财富。

en: The sobering aspect of Allen's book is that we have no one else to blame for our present condition except ourselves. (21 words)
cn: 此书令人深思的一点是：对于我们当下所处的环境，除了我们自己，没有任何人可以抱怨。

en: (50) The upside is the possibilities contained in knowing that everything is up to us; where before we were experts in the array of limitations, now we become authorities of what is possible. (34 words)
cn: （50）积极的一面是，既然万事都取决于我们，那么就有无限的可能。以前，我们能够熟练地应对种种局限；现在，我们把握着所有的可能性。

en: Since the days of Aristotle, a search for universal principles has characterized the scientific enterprise. (15 words)
cn: 从亚里士多德时代开始，探求普遍规则已经成为科学研究的典型特征。

en: In some ways, this quest for commonalities defines science. (9 words)
cn: 从某些方面来讲，科学就是对共性的追寻。

en: Newton's laws of motion and Darwinian evolution each bind a host of different phenomena into a single explicatory framework. (20 words)
cn: 无论是牛顿的运动定律还是达尔文的进化理论，都将不同的现象归结为单一的解释。

en: (46) In physics, one approach takes this impulse for unification to its extreme, and seeks a theory of everything—a single generative equation for all we see. (27 words)
cn: （46）在物理学领域，有一种做法将这种追求一致的冲动推向了极端，它试图找出一种万有理论——用一个生成等式概括我们所看到的一切。

en: It is becoming less clear, however, that such a theory would be a simplification, given the dimensions and universes that it might entail. (24 words)
cn: 然而，该理论是否可以称为一种简化已变得越发不确定，因为它可能包括了维度和星系。

en: Nonetheless, unification of sorts remains a major goal. (8 words)
cn: 尽管如此，追求万物的统一仍然是主要的目标。

en: This tendency in the natural sciences has long been evident in the social sciences too. (16 words)
cn: 自然科学中的这种倾向在社会科学领域也一直很明显。

en: (47) Here, Darwinism seems to offer justification, for if all humans share common origins, it seems reasonable to suppose that cultural diversity could also be traced to more constrained beginnings. (29 words)
cn: （47）在此，达尔文主义似乎给出了一种合理的说法，因为如果全人类的起源相同，那么似乎有理由认为文化多样性也可以追溯到更有限的开端。

en: Just as the bewildering variety of human courtship rituals might all be considered to be forms of sexual selection, perhaps the world's languages, music, social and religious customs and even history are governed by universal features. (34 words)
cn: 正如人类令人眼花缭乱的求婚仪式可以看作两性选择的形式一般，或许世界上的语言、音乐、社会及宗教习俗，甚至是历史都由统一的特征所支配。

en: (48) To filter out what is unique from what is shared might enable us to understand how complex cultural behaviour arose and what guides it in evolutionary or cognitive terms. (29 words)
cn: （48）如果将个别因素从共性中剔除，我们或许能够理解复杂的文化行为是如何产生的，以及是何种因素在进化或是认知方面引导了这种行为。

en: That, at least, is the hope. (6 words)
cn: 至少，那是一种希望。

en: But a comparative study of linguistic traits published online today supplies a reality check. (14 words)
cn: 但如今，一份发表在网络上的语言特征比较研究提醒人们审视现实。

en: Russell Gray at the University of Auckland and his colleagues consider the evolution of grammars in the light of two previous attempts to find universality in language. (28 words)
cn: 基于前人对语言普遍性研究的两次尝试的结果，奥克兰大学的拉塞尔·格雷和同事认真研究了语法的演化。

en: The most famous of these efforts was initiated by Noam Chomsky, who suggested that humans are born with an innate language-acquisition capacity that dictates a universal grammar. (27 words)
cn: 在该研究领域，最为著名的当属诺姆·乔姆斯基，他认为人类天生就具有语言习得能力，如此便可以形成一种普遍的语法。

en: A few generative rules are then sufficient to unfold the entire fundamental structure of a language, which is why children can learn it so quickly. (27 words)
cn: 这样，一些生成法便足以解构语言的全部基础结构，这也正是孩子们能够迅速学习语言的原因所在。

en: (49) The second, by Joshua Greenberg, takes a more empirical approach to universality, identifying traits (particularly in word order) shared by many languages, which are considered to represent biases that result from cognitive constraints. (34 words)
cn: （49）做出又一贡献的是乔舒亚·格林伯格，他运用了更为鲜明的经验主义方法来研究这种普遍性，鉴别出多种语言的共同特征（特别是在词序上的特征），而人们认为这些特征代表了由认知制约所产生的偏见。

en: Gray and his colleagues have put them to the test by examining four family trees that between them represent more than 2,000 languages. (24 words)
cn: 格雷和他的同事通过对代表了2000多种语言的四种语言谱系进行考查，检验了这两种普遍性法则。

en: (50) Chomsky's grammar should show patterns of language change that are independent of the family tree or the pathway tracked through it, whereas Greenbergian universality predicts strong co-dependencies between particular types of word-order relations. (34 words)
cn: （50）乔姆斯基的语法应该表明语言变化的模式，这些模式并没有受到语言谱系或贯穿谱系的路径的影响，然而格林伯格的普遍性理论则认为在特定词序位置关系之间有着紧密的依附关系。

en: Neither of these patterns is borne out by the analysis, suggesting that the structures of the languages are lineage-specific and not governed by universals. (24 words)
cn: 这些模式都无法靠分析证实，这表明语言结构具有语系的特定性，并不受普遍性支配。

en: It is speculated that gardens arise from a basic human need in the individuals who made them: the need for creative expression. (22 words)
cn: 人们猜测，花园的产生源自建造者的基本需求：对创造性表达的需求。

en: There is no doubt that gardens evidence an irrepressible urge to create, express, fashion, and beautify and that self-expression is a basic human urge; (23 words)
cn: 毫无疑问，花园代表了人们对创造、表达、设计和修饰的一种难以抑制的诉求，同样，自我表达本身也是人类的基本诉求；

en: (46) yet when one looks at the photographs of the gardens created by the homeless, it strikes one that, for all their diversity of styles, these gardens speak of various other fundamental urges, beyond that of decoration and creative expression. (38 words)
cn: （46）然而，当我们看到这样的照片，看到那些无家可归者所创建的花园之时，感到了深深的震撼：尽管它们风格多样，但这些花园道出了人类还有很多其他的根本需求，而非停留在装饰美化或是创造性表达上。

en: One of these urges has to do with creating a state of peace in the midst of turbulence, a "still point of the turning world," to borrow a phrase from T. S. Eliot. (31 words)
cn: 其中的一项诉求与创造一种闹中取静的状态有关，借用T.S.艾略特的说法就是：一个"旋转世界的静止点"。

en: (47) A sacred place of peace, however crude it may be, is a distinctly human need, as opposed to shelter, which is a distinctly animal need. (26 words)
cn: （47）它是一个和平的圣地，不管它有多么粗陋，但它明显是人类所需要的，与收容所迥异，收容所明显是动物的需求。

en: This distinction is so much so that where the latter is lacking, as it is for these unlikely gardeners, the former becomes all the more urgent. (26 words)
cn: 这种区别如此之大，以至于当后者（住所）缺失时——就像这些看似不可能成为园丁的人所面临的那样——人们对前者（安宁圣地）的需求就变得愈发迫切。

en: Composure is a state of mind made possible by the structuring of one's relation to one's environment. (18 words)
cn: 安宁是一种心境，通过构造个人与环境的关系可以达到这一状态。

en: (48) The gardens of the homeless, which are in effect homeless gardens, introduce form into an urban environment where it either didn't exist or was not discernible as such. (28 words)
cn: （48）无家可归者的花园实际上是无人问津的花园，它们是被引入城市环境中的一种建筑类型。在城市环境下，这种建筑风格要么本来就不存在，要么人们注意不到它的存在。

en: In so doing they give composure to a segment of the inarticulate environment in which they take their stand. (20 words)
cn: 在这种情况下，他们为其所处的难以用语言表达的环境带来了些许安宁。

en: Another urge or need that these gardens appear to respond to, or to arise from, is so intrinsic that we are barely ever conscious of its abiding claims on us. (29 words)
cn: 这些花园似乎还反映出，或者说它们还源自另一种根深蒂固的诉求或是需求，以至于我们几乎不会意识到它们对我们长久以来的索取。

en: When we are deprived of green, of plants, of trees, (10 words)
cn: 当我们丧失绿地、植物、树木时，

en: (49) most of us give in to a demoralization of spirit which we usually blame on some psychological conditions, until one day we find ourselves in a garden and feel the oppression vanish as if by magic. (36 words)
cn: （49）多数人会变得精神萎靡，我们通常把这种萎靡归咎于某种心理状态，直到有一天，我们发现自己身处花园，竟奇迹般地感到压力全无。

en: In most of the homeless gardens of New York City the actual cultivation of plants is unfeasible, yet even so the compositions often seem to represent attempts to call forth the spirit of plant and animal life, if only symbolically, through a clumplike arrangement of materials, an introduction of colors, small pools of water, and a frequent presence of petals or leaves as well as of stuffed animals. (65 words)
cn: 在纽约市的大部分流浪者花园里，实际的植物培育是不可能的，但是即便如此，这些人造景观似乎总是体现了人们唤起植物与动物生命力的尝试，哪怕只是象征性地借助树丛的布置、色彩的运用，以及小小的水潭、随处可见的花瓣或树叶，还有各种动物玩偶。

en: On display here are various fantasy elements whose reference, at some basic level, seems to be the natural world. (19 words)
cn: 这里所展现的是各式奇思妙想的元素，从某种本源的角度看，它们似乎借鉴的是大自然。

en: (50) It is this implicit or explicit reference to nature that fully justifies the use of the word garden, though in a "liberated" sense, to describe these synthetic constructions. (29 words)
cn: （50）正是这种与大自然之间或明或暗的关联使花园这个词——虽然指的是"广义上的"花园——恰如其分地形容了这些人造建筑。

en: In them we can see biophilia—a yearning for contact with nonhuman life—assuming uncanny representational forms. (17 words)
cn: 在这些花园中，我们能看到亲生命性——一种渴望与非人类生命相接触的天性——呈现出奇异的具象形式。

en: Music means different things to different people and sometimes even different things to the same person at different moments of his life. (23 words)
cn: 不同的人对音乐有不同的感受，但有时候，即使是同一个人，在生命的不同时刻，也会对音乐有不同的感受。

en: It might be poetic, philosophical, sensual, or mathematical, but in any case it must, in my view, have something to do with the soul of the human being. (28 words)
cn: 音乐蕴含着诗意，具有哲理，它能精确表达，直指人心。但是无论如何，在我看来，音乐都必须与人的灵魂相通。

en: Hence it is metaphysical; but the means of expression is purely and exclusively physical: sound. (15 words)
cn: 因此，它也具有形而上学的概念，但其表达方式则完完全全是物理学的方式：声音。

en: I believe it is precisely this permanent coexistence of metaphysical message through physical means that is the strength of music. (21 words)
cn: 我相信，正是这种通过物理学的方式与形而上学的永恒共存，才是音乐的力量所在。

en: (46) It is also the reason why when we try to describe music with words, all we can do is articulate our reactions to it, and not grasp music itself. (28 words)
cn: （46）这也是为什么我们在试图用语言来描述音乐时，只能说清楚对音乐的感受，而不能理解音乐本身。

en: Beethoven's importance in music has been principally defined by the revolutionary nature of his compositions. (14 words)
cn: 贝多芬具有革命性意义的作品奠定了他在音乐中的地位。

en: He freed music from hitherto prevailing conventions of harmony and structure. (11 words)
cn: 他使音乐摆脱了早期盛行的和声与结构的束缚。

en: Sometimes I feel in his late works a will to break all signs of continuity. (16 words)
cn: 有时候，我在他晚期的作品中感受到，他想要打破作品的整体性。

en: The music is abrupt and seemingly disconnected, as in the last piano sonata. (14 words)
cn: 正如他的最后一首钢琴奏鸣曲，曲风突变，似乎还有点脱节。

en: In musical expression, he did not feel restrained by the weight of convention. (13 words)
cn: 他不受传统的约束，能够自由自在地表达音乐。

en: (47) By all accounts he was a freethinking person, and a courageous one, and I find courage an essential quality for the understanding, let alone the performance, of his works. (29 words)
cn: （47）人们普遍认为他是个思想自由的人，是个勇敢无畏的人。我发现勇敢这一特征是理解他作品的关键，更不必说是演奏其作品的关键。

en: This courageous attitude in fact becomes a requirement for the performers of Beethoven's music. (13 words)
cn: 事实上，演奏贝多芬作品的人也应该具有这种勇敢的态度。

en: His compositions demand the performer to show courage, for example in the use of dynamics. (15 words)
cn: 他的作品需要演奏者展现自己的勇气，例如在力度的使用上。

en: (48) Beethoven's habit of increasing the volume with an extreme intensity and then abruptly following it with a sudden soft passage was only rarely used by composers before him. (29 words)
cn: （48）贝多芬习惯用极其强烈的情绪来提高乐曲的音量，然后又出人意料地突然跟上一个轻柔的乐段，他之前的作曲家极少采用这种作曲方式。

en: Beethoven was a deeply political man in the broadest sense of the word. (14 words)
cn: 在更宽泛的意义上说，贝多芬是一个非常关心政治的人。

en: He was not interested in daily politics, but concerned with questions of moral behavior and the larger questions of right and wrong affecting the entire society. (27 words)
cn: 他不是对日常的政治活动感兴趣，而是关注道德行为的问题，关注影响整个社会的是与非等更大的问题。

en: (49) Especially significant was his view of freedom, which, for him, was associated with the rights and responsibilities of the individual: he advocated freedom of thought and of personal expression. (29 words)
cn: （49）尤为重要的是他对自由的观点，对他而言，自由是与个人的权利和责任联系在一起的；他倡导思想自由和个人言论自由。

en: Beethoven's music tends to move from chaos to order as if order were an imperative of human existence. (19 words)
cn: 贝多芬的音乐总是从混乱到有序，似乎有序成了人类存在的必需一样。

en: For him, order does not result from forgetting or ignoring the disorders that plague our existence; order is a necessary development, an improvement that may lead to the Greek ideal of spiritual elevation. (33 words)
cn: 在他看来，有序并不是源自忘记或者忽略那些影响我们存在的无序状态；有序是一种必要的发展，一种改进，它可以使我们的精神提升到一种希腊式的理想状态。

en: It is not by chance that the Funeral March is not the last movement of the Eroica Symphony, but the second, so that suffering does not have the last word. (30 words)
cn: 《葬礼进行曲》不是《英雄交响曲》的最后一个乐章，而是第二个乐章，这并非偶然，这样一来，痛苦就不会成为最终的裁定。

en: (50) One could interpret much of the work of Beethoven by saying that suffering is inevitable, but the courage to fight it renders life worth living. (26 words)
cn: （50）我们可以这样来解读贝多芬的大多数作品：痛苦是难以避免的，但是与之相抗争的勇气使得生命值得继续。

en: Within the span of a hundred years, in the seventeenth and early eighteenth centuries, a tide of emigration—one of the great folk wanderings of history—swept from Europe to America. (30 words)
cn: 从17世纪到18世纪早期，在这一百年的时间里，移民潮从欧洲横扫至美洲——这是伟大的民族迁移史。

en: (46) This movement, driven by powerful and diverse motivations, built a nation out of a wilderness and, by its nature, shaped the character and destiny of an uncharted continent. (27 words)
cn: （46）受到各种强大的动机所驱使，这场运动在荒野中创建了一个国家；本质使然，它也塑造了这片未知大陆的性格和命运。

en: (47) The United States is the product of two principal forces—the immigration of European peoples with their varied ideas, customs, and national characteristics and the impact of a new country which modified these traits. (31 words)
cn: （47）有两股主要力量形成了美国：一是欧洲移民带来的各式思想、风俗和民族特征，二是上述特征又在这个新国家的影响之下发生了改变。

en: Of necessity, colonial America was a projection of Europe. (9 words)
cn: 殖民时期的美国必然是欧洲的映射。

en: Across the Atlantic came successive groups of Englishmen, Frenchmen, Germans, Scots, Irishmen, Dutchmen, Swedes, and many others who attempted to transplant their habits and traditions to the new world. (28 words)
cn: 接连跨过大西洋到达美洲的民族包括英吉利、法国、德意志、苏格兰、爱尔兰、荷兰、瑞典和许多其他国家的人，他们试图把本国的习惯和传统带到这个新大陆。

en: (48) But the force of geographic conditions peculiar to America, the interplay of the varied national groups upon one another, and the sheer difficulty of maintaining old-world ways in a raw, new continent caused significant changes. (33 words)
cn: （48）但是，美国所特有的地理条件、不同民族间的相互影响以及在这片原始的新大陆上维持旧秩序的艰难带来了巨大的变化。

en: These changes were gradual and at first scarcely visible. (9 words)
cn: 这些变化是渐进的并且最初是不明显的。

en: But the result was a new social pattern which, although it resembled European society in many ways, had a character that was distinctly American. (24 words)
cn: 但结果是形成了一个具有典型的美国特征的新的社会形态，尽管它在许多方面仍有欧洲社会的影子。

en: (49) The first shiploads of immigrants bound for the territory which is now the United States crossed the Atlantic more than a hundred years after the 15th-and-16th-century explorations of North America. (30 words)
cn: （49）在15世纪和16世纪人们对北美洲进行了持续的探索，又过了一百多年，第一艘满载移民的航船跨过大西洋驶向这片土地，即现在的美国。

en: In the meantime, thriving Spanish colonies had been established in Mexico, the West Indies, and South America. (18 words)
cn: 在此期间，蓬勃发展的西班牙殖民地已经在墨西哥、西印度群岛和南美地区建立起来了。

en: These travelers to North America came in small, unmercifully overcrowded craft. (11 words)
cn: 这些去北美地区的人要坐着十分拥挤的小船。

en: During their six-to twelve-week voyage, they survived on barely enough food allotted to them. (15 words)
cn: 在6到12周的航行中，他们依靠着微薄的口粮度日。

en: Many of the ships were lost in storms, many passengers died of disease, and infants rarely survived the journey. (19 words)
cn: 许多船只在暴风雨中迷失，许多人死于疾病，婴幼儿很少能在旅途中存活。

en: Sometimes storms blew the vessels far off their course, and often calm brought unbearably long delay. (16 words)
cn: 时而兴起的大风暴会让船远离他们的航线行驶，时常的风平浪静会带给他们的旅途难以忍受的长时延期。

en: To the anxious travelers the sight of the American shore brought almost inexpressible relief. (14 words)
cn: 对于这些急切不安的旅行者来说，能看到美国口岸给他们带来几乎无法表达的解脱。

en: Said one recorder of events, "The air at twelve leagues' distance smelt as sweet as a new-blown garden." (19 words)
cn: 据一位记事者说："这12里格距离的空气如同开满鲜花的花园一样甘甜。"

en: The colonists' first glimpse of the new land was a sight of dense woods. (14 words)
cn: 殖民者最先看到的是新大陆上郁郁葱葱的树木。

en: (50) The virgin forest with its richness and variety of trees was a real treasure-house which extended from Maine all the way down to Georgia. (25 words)
cn: （50）原始的森林，有着种类繁多的林木，从缅因州往南一直绵延至乔治亚州，的确是一座天然的宝库。

en: Here was abundant fuel and lumber. (6 words)
cn: 这里有丰富的燃料和木材。

en: Here was the raw material of houses and furniture, ships and potash, dyes and naval stores. (17 words)
cn: 这些是制作房屋、家具和船只，以及调制钾碱、染料和松脂制品的原材料。

en: Mental health is our birthright. (5 words)
cn: 心理健康是我们生而有之的权利。

en: (46) We don't have to learn how to be mentally healthy; it is built into us in the same way that our bodies know how to heal a cut or mend a broken bone. (32 words)
cn: （46）我们没有必要去学习如何保持心理健康；心理健康是与生俱来的，正如我们的身体知道如何让伤口愈合，断骨复原。

en: Mental health can't be learned, only reawakened. (7 words)
cn: 心理健康学不到，只能重新唤起心理上的健康。

en: It is like the immune system of the body, which under stress or through lack of nutrition or exercise can be weakened, but which never leaves us. (25 words)
cn: 它就像身体的免疫系统一样，若承受压力，或缺乏营养，缺少锻炼，就会变得很虚弱，但是它并没有离开我们。

en: When we don't understand the value of mental health and we don't know how to gain access to it, mental health will remain hidden from us. (25 words)
cn: 如果我们不懂得心理健康的重要性，或者如果我们不知道如何去获取心理健康，它就会继续隐藏。

en: (47) Our mental health doesn't really go anywhere; like the sun behind a cloud, it can be temporarily hidden from view, but it is fully capable of being restored in an instant. (31 words)
cn: （47）心理健康实际上并没有消失；就像乌云背后的太阳，它可能暂时被遮住而看不到了，但是它完全能够立刻重焕光芒。

en: Mental health is the seed that contains self-esteem—confidence in ourselves and an ability to trust in our common sense. (19 words)
cn: 心理健康是一颗种子，包含了自尊（即自信）以及常识判断的能力。

en: It allows us to have perspective on our lives—the ability to not take ourselves too seriously, to laugh at ourselves, to see the bigger picture, and to see that things will work out. (32 words)
cn: 它使我们着眼于自己的生活——这是一种能力，不苛求自己，调侃自己，看得更远，且明白事情终将圆满解决。

en: It's a form of innate or unlearned optimism. (8 words)
cn: 它是一种固有的乐观精神，一种不用学习就具备的乐观精神。

en: (48) Mental health allows us to view others with sympathy if they are having troubles, with kindness if they are in pain, and with unconditional love no matter who they are. (30 words)
cn: （48）心理健康可以让我们在别人陷入麻烦之时，同情他人；在别人痛苦之时，友善待人；无论是谁，都能给予其无条件的关爱。

en: Mental health is the source of creativity for solving problems, resolving conflict, making our surroundings more beautiful, managing our home life, or coming up with a creative business idea or invention to make our lives easier. (34 words)
cn: 心理健康可以激发创造力，从而解决问题，化解冲突，让我们周围的环境更美好，经营好家庭生活，或者激发我们的商业创意或商业发现来让我们的生活更轻松。

en: It gives us patience for ourselves and toward others as well as patience while driving, catching a fish, working on our car, or raising a child. (25 words)
cn: 它可以让我们耐心地对自己和他人，还能让我们在开车、修车、钓鱼，或者养育孩子的时候都有耐心。

en: It allows us to see the beauty that surrounds us each moment in nature, in culture, in the flow of our daily lives. (22 words)
cn: 它可以让我们时时刻刻发现周围的美，在大自然中，在文化中，在我们的日常生活中，到处都可以看到美的存在。

en: (49) Although mental health is the cure-all for living our lives, it is perfectly ordinary as you will see that it has been there to direct you through all your difficult decisions. (30 words)
cn: （49）尽管拥有健康的心理是我们生活中的灵丹妙药，但它却非常普通；你会发现，它一直都在，指引你渡过艰难，做出选择。

en: It has been available even in the most mundane of life situations to show you right from wrong, good from bad, friend from foe. (24 words)
cn: 即使在最为平凡的生活中，你也可以发现它的存在，让你辨明是非，了解善恶，分清敌友。

en: Mental health has commonly been called conscience, instinct, wisdom, common sense, or the inner voice. (16 words)
cn: 心理健康通常也被称作良知、本能、智慧、常识、或者是内心的声音。

en: We think of it simply as a healthy and helpful flow of intelligent thought. (14 words)
cn: 我们认为它只不过就是一种健康并且有益的睿智思维。

en: (50) As you will come to see, knowing that mental health is always available and knowing to trust it allow us to slow down to the moment and live life happily. (29 words)
cn: （50）你会慢慢发现，知道心理健康总是唾手可得，还要知道去相信自己健康的心理，这可以使我们及时放慢生活的节奏，过上幸福的生活。

en: The growth of the use of English as the world's primary language for international communication has obviously been continuing for several decades. (21 words)
cn: 英语是世界上国际间交流的主要语言，英语的使用明显在不断增加，且持续达数十年。

en: (46) But even as the number of English speakers expands further there are signs that the global predominance of the language may fade within the foreseeable future. (26 words)
cn: （46）但即使是说英语的人数在进一步扩大，仍有迹象表明，英语的全球性优势地位在不久的将来会逐渐减弱。

en: Complex international, economic, technological and cultural changes could start to diminish the leading position of English as the language of the world market, and UK interests which enjoy advantage from the breadth of English usage would consequently face new pressures. (36 words)
cn: 国际、经济、技术和文化等方面的复杂变革会削弱英语作为全球市场交流语言的领先地位。那些从英语广泛使用中享有优势的英国利益集团也因此会面对新的压力。

en: Those realistic possibilities are highlighted in the study presented by David Graddol. (12 words)
cn: 戴维·格兰多的研究强调了这些现实可能出现的情况。

en: (47) His analysis should therefore end any self-contentedness among those who may believe that the global position of English is so stable that the young generations of the United Kingdom do not need additional language capabilities. (32 words)
cn: （47）有些人可能会认为英语在世界上的地位很稳定，英国的年轻人不需要掌握其他语言；格兰多的分析因此应该会打消那些人的自满情绪。

en: David Graddol concludes that monoglot English graduates face a bleak economic future as qualified multilingual youngsters from other countries are proving to have a competitive advantage over their British counterparts in global companies and organisations. (32 words)
cn: 戴维·格兰多断定，只会说英语这一门语言的毕业生前途暗淡，然而来自其他国家会说多门语言的年轻人会有明显的竞争优势，他们在国际公司或国际组织中会把来自英国的对手比下去。

en: Alongside that, (48) many countries are introducing English into the primary-school curriculum but British schoolchildren and students do not appear to be gaining greater encouragement to achieve fluency in other languages. (28 words)
cn: 同时，（48）很多国家正把英语纳入小学课程，但是似乎并没有人给予英国的中小学生更多的鼓励，让他们能去熟练地掌握其他语言。

en: If left to themselves, such trends will diminish the relative strength of the English language in international education markets as the demand for educational resources in languages, such as Spanish, Arabic or Mandarin grows and international business process outsourcing in other languages such as Japanese, French and German, spreads. (44 words)
cn: 如果听之任之，这样的趋势会降低英语在国际教育市场的相对优势，而对像西班牙语、阿拉伯语或者汉语等教育资源的需求就会增加，国际商务中使用日语、法语和德语等语言进行的业务流程外包也会增加。

en: (49) The changes identified by David Graddol all present clear and major challenges to the UK's providers of English language teaching to people of other countries and to broader education business sectors. (29 words)
cn: （49）戴维·格兰多指出的这些变化明显给教授其他国家的人学习英语的英国教育机构，还给广大从事教育行业的公司，都带来了巨大的挑战。

en: The English language teaching sector directly earns nearly £1.3 billion for the UK in invisible exports and our other education related exports earn up to £10 billion a year more. (29 words)
cn: 英语教育机构以无形出口的形式直接给英国带来了近13亿英镑的收入，其他与教育相关的出口收入则达到每年100亿英镑之多。

en: As the international education market expands, the recent slowdown in the numbers of international students studying in the main English-speaking countries is likely to continue, especially if there are no effective strategic policies to prevent such slippage. (34 words)
cn: 随着国际教育市场的扩大，在几个说英语的主要国家留学的国际学生数量最近出现下滑，尤其是如果还没有阻止这种下滑趋势的有效战略措施，这种趋势仍然有可能持续。

en: The anticipation of possible shifts in demand provided by this study is significant: (13 words)
cn: 该研究预测到需求可能发生变化，这一点意义非凡。

en: (50) It gives a basis for all organisations which seek to promote the learning and use of English, a basis for planning to meet the possibilities of what could be a very different operating environment. (32 words)
cn: （50）这给试图推广英语学习和使用的所有机构提供了一个制定规划的基本依据，据此去应对可能会出现的一个完全不同的运行环境。

en: That is a necessary and practical approach. (7 words)
cn: 这是一个必不可少的务实的措施。

en: In this as in much else, those who wish to influence the future must prepare for it. (17 words)
cn: 因此，无论从哪方面来说，那些想要影响未来的人应该为此做好准备。

en: Shakespeare's lifetime was coincident with a period of extraordinary activity and achievement in the drama. (14 words)
cn: 莎士比亚的一生正逢戏剧发展繁荣昌盛并取得伟大成就的时期。

en: (46) By the date of his birth Europe was witnessing the passing of the religious drama, and the creation of new forms under the incentive of classical tragedy and comedy. (28 words)
cn: （46）在他出生之际，宗教戏剧在欧洲正在消失，在古典悲剧和喜剧的激发下，各种新形式的戏剧产生了。

en: These new forms were at first mainly written by scholars and performed by amateurs, but in England, as everywhere else in western Europe, the growth of a class of professional actors was threatening to make the drama popular, whether it should be new or old, classical or medieval, literary or farcical. (46 words)
cn: 这些新的戏剧种类最开始主要是由学者们进行创作，业余演员表演的。但是在英国，正如西欧其他地方一样，无论戏剧种类新旧，无论是古典戏剧还是中世纪戏剧，文学剧还是滑稽剧，专业演员这一群体的壮大都可能会使戏剧越来越通俗。

en: Court, school, organizations of amateurs, and the traveling actors were all rivals in supplying a widespread desire for dramatic entertainment; and (47) no boy who went to a grammar school could be ignorant that the drama was a form of literature which gave glory to Greece and Rome and might yet bring honor to England. (48 words)
cn: 宫廷、学校、业余团体以及巡回表演的演员，都竞相对戏剧演出提出广泛需求。并且（47）戏剧是一种文学形式，它曾给希腊和罗马带来荣耀，早晚也会给英国带来荣耀；任何一个上过文法学校的学生都不会不知道这一点。

en: When Shakespeare was twelve years old the first public playhouse was built in London. (14 words)
cn: 在莎士比亚12岁那年，伦敦建立了第一座公共剧场。

en: For a time literature showed no interest in this public stage. (11 words)
cn: 有一段时间，文学界对这种公共舞台表演毫无兴趣。

en: Plays aiming at literary distinction were written for schools or court, or for the choir boys of St. Paul's and the royal chapel, who, however, gave plays in public as well as at court. (32 words)
cn: 追求文学造诣的戏剧不是写给学校和宫廷的，就是写给圣保罗和皇家教堂唱诗班的，然而这些唱诗班既在宫廷演出，也在公共剧场演出。

en: (48) But the professional companies prospered in their permanent theaters, and university men with literary ambitions were quick to turn to these theaters as offering a means of livelihood. (27 words)
cn: （48）但是专业剧团在他们的常驻剧院蓬勃发展，有文学抱负的大学生迅速热情地投身到这些剧院并以此谋生。

en: By the time that Shakespeare was twenty-five, Lyly, Peele, and Greene had made comedies that were at once popular and literary; Kyd had written a tragedy that crowded the pit; and Marlowe had brought poetry and genius to triumph on the common stage—where they had played no part since the death of Euripides. (50 words)
cn: 在莎士比亚25岁那年，李利、皮尔、格林都已创作了喜剧，一时间广受欢迎，文学造诣颇高；基德写的一部悲剧也让剧院人群爆满；而马洛已经把自己的诗歌和天赋展示在平凡的舞台上，成就非凡——在这样的舞台上，自从欧里庇得斯去世以来，这些人在戏剧上还从来没有获得过这样的地位。

en: (49) A native literary drama had been created, its alliance with the public playhouses established, and at least some of its great traditions had been begun. (23 words)
cn: （49）一种本土文学剧产生了，它与公共剧院的联合关系建立起来了，至少，其中一些伟大的传统也就此出现了。

en: The development of the Elizabethan drama for the next twenty-five years is of exceptional interest to students of literary history, for in this brief period we may trace the beginning, growth, blossoming, and decay of many kinds of plays, and of many great careers. (42 words)
cn: 文学史系的学生对接下来的25年中伊丽莎白时代戏剧的发展格外感兴趣，因为在这一短暂的时期内，我们可以追溯各种戏剧形式以及许多伟大事业的兴起、发展、繁荣和衰亡。

en: We are amazed today at the mere number of plays produced, as well as by the number of dramatists writing at the same time for this London of two hundred thousand inhabitants. (30 words)
cn: 如今，我们仅知道这段时期创作出来的戏剧数量就会感到惊讶；也会对当时伦敦20万居民中从事戏剧写作的人数感到诧异。

en: (50) To realize how great was the dramatic activity, we must remember further that hosts of plays have been lost, and that probably there is no author of note whose entire work has survived. (32 words)
cn: （50）要认识戏剧活动曾经的辉煌，我们必须要进一步知道，许多戏剧作品已不复存在，或许名家之作也难完整存留。

en: It was only after I started to write a weekly column about the medical journals, and began to read scientific papers from beginning to end, that I realised just how bad much of the medical literature frequently was. (35 words)
cn: 直到我开始撰写关于医学期刊的每周专栏，并开始从头到尾阅读科学论文之后，我才意识到很多医学文献通常是多么糟糕。

en: I came to recognise various signs of a bad paper: the kind of paper that purports to show that people who eat more than one kilo of broccoli a week were 1.17 times more likely than those who eat less to suffer late in life from pernicious anaemia. (42 words)
cn: 我逐渐发现了劣质论文的各种迹象：例如某些论文声称，每周食用超过一公斤西兰花的人在晚年患恶性贫血的几率是食用较少者的1.17倍。

en: (46) There is a great deal of this kind of nonsense in the medical journals which, when taken up by broadcasters and the lay press, generates both health scares and short-lived dietary enthusiasms. (31 words)
cn: （46）医学期刊中存在大量此类废话，这些废话一旦被广播公司和非专业媒体采用，就会引起健康恐慌和短暂的饮食热潮。

en: Why is so much bad science published? (7 words)
cn: 为什么会有这么多糟糕的科学成果发表？

en: A recent paper, titled "The Natural Selection of Bad Science", published on the Royal Society's open science website, attempts to answer this intriguing and important question. (27 words)
cn: 最近发表在英国皇家学会开放科学网站上的一篇题为《劣质科学的自然选择》的论文，试图回答这个有趣且重要的问题。

en: It says that the problem is not merely that people do bad science, but that our current system of career advancement positively encourages it. (24 words)
cn: 文章指出，问题不仅在于人们从事劣质科学研究，还在于我们目前的职业晋升制度在积极鼓励这种行为。

en: What is important is not truth, but publication, which has become almost an end in itself. (17 words)
cn: 重要的不是真理，而是发表文章，这几乎已经成为了目的本身。

en: There has been a kind of inflationary process at work: (10 words)
cn: 这种过程中存在一种通货膨胀现象：

en: (47) nowadays anyone applying for a research post has to have published twice the number of papers that would have been required for the same post only 10 years ago. (29 words)
cn: （47）如今，任何申请研究职位的人所发表的论文数量，必须是10年前同一职位要求的两倍。

en: Never mind the quality, then, count the number. (9 words)
cn: 那么，不要在意质量，数数数量就行了。

en: (48) Attempts have been made to curb this tendency, for example, by trying to incorporate some measure of quality as well as quantity into the assessment of an applicant's papers. (28 words)
cn: （48）人们已经做出努力来遏制这种趋势，例如，尝试在评价申请者的论文时，将质量和数量一并纳入考量范围。

en: This is the famed citation index, that is to say the number of times a paper has been quoted elsewhere in the scientific literature, the assumption being that an important paper will be cited more often than one of small account. (38 words)
cn: 这就是著名的引用指数，即一篇论文在其他科学文献中被引用的次数，其假设是一篇重要的论文会比一篇平庸的论文被引用得更多。

en: (49) This would be reasonable if it were not for the fact that scientists can easily arrange to cite themselves in their future publications, or get associates to do so for them in return for similar favours. (34 words)
cn: （49）如果不是因为科学家们可以轻易地在未来的出版物中引用自己，或者让同行通过相互勾结的方式互助引用，这种做法（引用指数）本是合理的。

en: Boiling down an individual's output to simple metrics, such as number of publications or journal impacts, entails considerable savings in time, energy and ambiguity. (24 words)
cn: 将个人的产出简化为简单的指标（如发表文章的数量或期刊影响力），确实可以节省大量的时间和精力，并减少歧义。

en: Unfortunately, the long-term costs of using simple quantitative metrics to assess researcher merit are likely to be quite great. (19 words)
cn: 不幸的是，使用简单的量化指标来衡量研究者的功绩，其长期代价可能是巨大的。

en: (50) If we are serious about ensuring that our science is both meaningful and reproducible, we must ensure that our institutions encourage that kind of science. (24 words)
cn: （50）如果我们真的想要确保我们的科学研究既有意义又可重复，那么我们必须确保我们的科研机构能够鼓励这种科学研究。

en: Following the explosion of creativity in Florence during the 14th century known as the Renaissance, the modern world saw a departure from what it had once known. (27 words)
cn: 继14世纪佛罗伦萨出现的被称为"文艺复兴"的创造力大爆发之后，现代世界见证了与以往认知的背离。

en: It turned from God and the authority of the Roman Catholic Church and instead favoured a more humanistic approach to being. (20 words)
cn: 它告别了上帝和罗马天主教会的权威，转而支持一种更具人文主义色彩的生存方式。

en: Renaissance ideas had spread throughout Europe well into the 17th century, with the arts and sciences flourishing extraordinarily among those with a more logical disposition. (25 words)
cn: 文艺复兴的思想一直传播到17世纪，遍布欧洲；在那些更具逻辑思维的人群中，艺术和科学呈现出非凡的繁荣景象。

en: (46) With the Church's teachings and ways of thinking being eclipsed by the Renaissance, the gap between the Medieval and modern periods had been bridged, leading to new and unexplored intellectual territories. (30 words)
cn: （46）随着教会的教义和思维方式在文艺复兴中黯然失色，中世纪与现代之间的鸿沟得以弥合，并通向了新的、尚未探索的知识领域。

en: During the Renaissance, the great minds of Nicolaus Copernicus, Johannes Kepler and Galileo Galilei demonstrated the power of scientific study and discovery. (20 words)
cn: 在文艺复兴时期，尼古拉·哥白尼、约翰内斯·开普勒和伽利略·伽利莱等伟大的思想家展示了科学研究和发现的力量。

en: (47) Before each of their revelations, many thinkers at the time had sustained more ancient ways of thinking, including the geocentric view that the Earth was at the centre of our universe. (30 words)
cn: （47）在他们的发现相继公布之前，当时的许多思想家仍坚持更古老的思维方式，包括地球是宇宙中心的"地心说"。

en: Copernicus theorized in 1543 that all of the planets that we knew of revolved not around the Earth, but the Sun, a system that was later upheld by Galileo at his own expense. (31 words)
cn: 1543年，哥白尼提出理论，认为我们所知的所有行星都不是绕着地球转，而是绕着太阳转；这一体系后来由伽利略冒着个人危险予以维护。

en: Offering up such a theory during a time of high tension between scientific and religious minds was branded as heresy, and any such heretics that continued to spread these lies were to be punished by imprisonment or even death. (37 words)
cn: 在科学与宗教思想高度紧张的时期提出这种理论被斥为异端邪说，任何继续传播这些谎言的异教徒都将受到监禁甚至处死的惩罚。

en: (48) Despite attempts by the Church to suppress this new generation of logicians and rationalists, more explanations for how the universe functioned were being made at a rate that the people could no longer ignore. (32 words)
cn: （48）尽管教会试图压制新一代的逻辑学家和理性主义者，但关于宇宙如何运作的解释正以人们无法再忽视的速度不断涌现。

en: It was with these great revelations that a new kind of philosophy founded in reason was born. (17 words)
cn: 正是凭借这些伟大的发现，一种建立在理性基础上的新型哲学诞生了。

en: The Church's long-standing dogma was losing the great battle for truth to rationalists and scientists. (15 words)
cn: 教会长期以来的教义在与理性主义者和科学家的真理大博弈中败下阵来。

en: This very fact embodied the new ways of thinking that swept through Europe during most of the 17th century. (19 words)
cn: 这一事实本身就体现了在17世纪大部分时间里席卷欧洲的新思维方式。

en: (49) As many took on the duty of trying to integrate reasoning and scientific philosophies into the world, the Renaissance was over and it was time for a new era—the Age of Reason. (31 words)
cn: （49）随着许多人担负起将理性思维和科学哲学融入世界的职责，文艺复兴宣告结束，一个新时代——"理性时代"来临了。

en: The 17th and 18th centuries were times of radical change and curiosity. (12 words)
cn: 17世纪和18世纪是发生剧变和充满好奇心的时代。

en: Scientific method, reductionism and the questioning of Church ideals was to be encouraged, as were ideas of liberty, tolerance and progress. (21 words)
cn: 科学方法、还原论以及对教会理想的质疑都受到了鼓励，自由、宽容和进步的思想也同样受到推崇。

en: (50) Such actions to seek knowledge and to understand what information we already knew were captured by the Latin phrase "sapere aude" or "dare to know", after Immanuel Kant used it in his essay "An Answer to the Question: What is Enlightenment?". (39 words)
cn: （50）在伊曼努尔·康德于其文章《对"什么是启蒙"这一问题的回答》中使用之后，这种追求知识并理解已知信息的行为被拉丁语短语"sapere aude"（即"敢于求知"）所概括。

en: It was the purpose and responsibility of great minds to go forth and seek out the truth, which they believed to be founded in knowledge. (25 words)
cn: 伟大的思想家们的目的和责任就是勇往直前、探求真理，他们相信真理是建立在知识的基础之上的。

en: World War II was the watershed event for higher education in modern Western societies. (14 words)
cn: 第二次世界大战是现代西方社会高等教育发展的分水岭。

en: (46) Those societies came out of the war with levels of enrollment that had been roughly constant at 3-5% of the relevant age groups during the decades before the war. (29 words)
cn: （46）这些社会从战争中走出时，其高校入学率在战前几十年间一直大致保持在相关年龄组的3%到5%。

en: But after the war, great social and political changes arising out of the successful war against Fascism created a growing demand in European and American economies for increasing numbers of graduates with more than a secondary school education. (35 words)
cn: 但战后，由于反法西斯战争的胜利，巨大的社会和政治变革使得欧美经济对具有高中以上学历的毕业生的需求日益增长。

en: (47) And the demand that rose in those societies for entry to higher education extended to groups and social classes that had not thought of attending a university before the war. (29 words)
cn: （47）这些社会中兴起的高等教育入学需求，延伸到了那些战前未曾想过上大学的群体和社会阶层。

en: These demands resulted in a very rapid expansion of the systems of higher education, beginning in the 1960s and developing very rapidly (though unevenly) during the 1970s and 1980s. (30 words)
cn: 这些需求导致了高等教育体系的迅速扩张，这一扩张始于20世纪60年代，并在70年代和80年代迅速发展（尽管并不均衡）。

en: The growth of higher education manifests itself in at least three quite different ways, and these in turn have given rise to different sets of problems. (25 words)
cn: 高等教育的增长至少以三种截然不同的方式表现出来，而这些方式又反过来引发了不同的问题。

en: There was first the rate of growth: (48) in many countries of Western Europe, the numbers of students in higher education doubled within five-year periods during the 1960s and doubled again in seven, eight, or 10 years by the middle of the 1970s. (39 words)
cn: 首先是增长率：（48）在许多西欧国家，高校学生人数在20世纪60年代期间以五年为周期翻了一番，到70年代中期又在七年、八年或十年内再次翻番。

en: Second, growth obviously affected the absolute size both of systems and individual institutions. (13 words)
cn: 其次，增长明显影响了高等教育体系和各院校的绝对规模。

en: And third, growth was reflected in changes in the proportion of the relevant age group enrolled in institutions of higher education. (21 words)
cn: 第三，增长体现在相关年龄组进入高等教育机构就读比例的变化上。

en: Each of these manifestations of growth carried its own peculiar problems in its wake. (14 words)
cn: 增长的每一种表现形式都随之带来了其特有的问题。

en: For example, a high growth rate placed great strains on the existing structures of governance, of administration, and above all of socialization. (22 words)
cn: 例如，高增长率给现有的治理结构、行政结构，尤其是社会化结构带来了巨大压力。

en: When a faculty or department grows from, say, five to 20 members within three or four years, (49) and when the new staff are predominantly young men and women fresh from postgraduate study, they largely define the norms of academic life in that faculty. (40 words)
cn: 当一个院系在三四年内从比如五名成员增长到二十名成员时，（49）而当新入职的教职工主要是刚完成研究生学业的年轻男女时，他们在很大程度上定义了该院系的学术生活规范。

en: And if the postgraduate student population also grows rapidly and there is loss of a close apprenticeship relationship between faculty members and students, the student culture becomes the chief socializing force for new postgraduate students, with consequences for the intellectual and academic life of the institution—this was seen in America as well as in France, Italy, West Germany, and Japan. (56 words)
cn: 如果研究生人数也快速增长，并且教师与学生之间紧密的师徒关系不复存在，那么学生文化就会成为新研究生社会化的主要力量，从而对该机构的学术和知识生活产生影响——这种情况既出现在美国，也出现在法国、意大利、西德和日本。

en: (50) High growth rates increased the chances for academic innovation; they also weakened the forms and processes by which teachers and students are admitted into a community of scholars during periods of stability or slow growth. (33 words)
cn: （50）高增长率增加了学术创新的机会；它们也削弱了在稳定期或缓慢增长期教师和学生得以进入学术共同体的形式和过程。

en: In the 1960s and 1970s, European universities saw marked changes in their governance arrangements, with the empowerment of junior faculty and to some degree of students as well. (28 words)
cn: 在20世纪60年代和70年代，欧洲大学的治理安排发生了显著变化，初级教职员工被赋予权力，学生在某种程度上也获得了权力。

en: Between 1807 and 1814 the Iberian Peninsula (comprising Spain and Portugal) was the scene of a titanic and merciless struggle. (18 words)
cn: 1807年至1814年间，在伊比利亚半岛（包括西班牙和葡萄牙），曾上演了一场激烈而又残酷的斗争。

en: It took place on many different planes: between Napoleon's French Army and the angry inhabitants; between the British, ever keen to exacerbate the emperor's difficulties, and the marshals sent from Paris to try to keep them in check; between new forces of science and meritocracy and old ones of conservatism and birth. (48 words)
cn: 这场斗争发生在许多不同的层面：在拿破仑的法国军队和愤怒的当地居民之间；在热衷于给皇帝制造困难的英国人与从巴黎派来试图牵制他们的元帅之间；在科学和精英管理体制下的新势力与保守主义和出身论的旧势力之间。

en: (46) It was also, and this is unknown even to many people well read about the period, a battle between those who made codes and those who broke them. (27 words)
cn: （46）这也是一场密码编制者与破译者之间的战争，这一点甚至许多熟知那一时期历史的人也并不了解。

en: I first discovered the Napoleonic code-breaking battle a few years ago when I was reading Sir Charles Oman's epic History of the Peninsular War. (22 words)
cn: 几年前，当我阅读查尔斯·欧曼爵士的史诗巨作《半岛战争史》时，第一次发现了拿破仑时期的密码破译之战。

en: In volume V he had attached an appendix, "The Scovell Ciphers." (11 words)
cn: 在第五卷中，他附上了一个附录——"斯科维尔密码"。

en: (47) It listed many documents in code that had been captured from the French Army of Spain, and whose secrets had been revealed by the work of one George Scovell, an officer in British headquarters. (33 words)
cn: （47）附录中列出了许多从驻扎在西班牙的法军手中缴获的加密文件，这些文件的秘密已通过英国司令部的一名军官乔治·斯科维尔的工作被揭示出来。

en: Oman rated Scovell's significance highly, but at the same time, the general nature of his History meant that (48) he could not analyze carefully what this obscure officer may or may not have contributed to that great struggle between nations or indeed tell us anything much about the man himself. (45 words)
cn: 欧曼对斯科维尔的重要性评价很高，但与此同时，他这部《战争史》的宏观性质意味着（48）他无法仔细分析这位名不见经传的军官是否对那场国家间的伟大战役做出过贡献，也无法详细告诉我们关于这个人本身的更多信息。

en: Keen to read more, I was surprised to find that Oman's appendix, published in 1914, was the only considered thing that had been written about this secret war. (26 words)
cn: 我渴望了解更多，却惊讶地发现欧曼于1914年出版的附录，竟是关于这场秘密战争唯一有分量的论述。

en: I became convinced that this story was every bit as exciting and significant as that of Enigma and the breaking of German codes in the Second World War. (27 words)
cn: 我开始确信，这个故事如同第二次世界大战中破解德国密码的"恩尼格玛"故事一样令人兴奋且意义重大。

en: The question was, could it be told? (7 words)
cn: 问题是，这个故事能被讲述出来吗？

en: Studying Scovell's papers at the Public Record Office, London, I found that he had left an extensive journal and copious notes about his work in the Peninsula. (25 words)
cn: 在伦敦公共档案馆研究斯科维尔的论文时，我发现他留下了大量关于他在半岛工作的日记和笔记。

en: What was more, many original French dispatches had been preserved in this collection, which, I realized, was priceless. (17 words)
cn: 此外，馆藏中还保存了许多法国公文的原稿，我意识到这些都是无价之宝。

en: (49) There may have been many spies and intelligence officers during the Napoleonic Wars, but it is usually extremely difficult to find the material they actually provided or worked on. (28 words)
cn: （49）拿破仑战争时期可能有很多间谍和情报官员，但通常很难找到他们实际提供或处理过的材料。

en: As I researched Scovell's story, I found far more of interest besides his intelligence work. (14 words)
cn: 当我研究斯科维尔的故事时，除了他的情报工作外，我还发现了更多有趣的事情。

en: His status in Lord Wellington's headquarters and the recognition given to him for his work were bound up with the class politics of the Army at the time. (26 words)
cn: 他在惠灵顿勋爵司令部的地位以及他的工作所获得的认可，与当时军队的阶级政治密不可分。

en: His story of self-improvement and hard work would make a fascinating biography in its own right, but represents something more than that. (21 words)
cn: 他奋斗自强的故事本身就可以成为一部引人入胜的传记，但其意义远不止于此。

en: (50) Just as the code-breaking has its wider relevance in the struggle for Spain, so his attempts to make his way up the promotion ladder speak volumes about British society. (28 words)
cn: （50）正如密码破译在争夺西班牙的斗争中具有更广泛的意义一样，他力求晋升的尝试也充分说明了当时的英国社会。

en: There has been some exploration around the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in digital marketing. (15 words)
cn: 人工智能在数字营销领域中的应用，人们已进行了一些探索。

en: For example, AI can be used to analyse what type of advertising content or copy would be appropriate to "speak" to a specific target customer group by revealing information about trends and preferences through the analysis of big data. (35 words)
cn: 例如，通过分析大数据，显示出有关消费趋势和偏好的信息，人工智能可以用来分析哪种类型的广告内容或文字适合向特定的目标客户群体"传达"。

en: (46) AI can also be used to identify the lifestyle choices of customers regarding their hobbies, favourite celebrities, music choices, and fashions to provide unique content in marketing messages put out through social media. (31 words)
cn: （46）人工智能也能用来识别顾客选择的生活方式，比如其业余爱好、最喜欢的名人、选择的音乐、还有时尚等方面，从而在推送通过社交媒体发布的营销信息时提供独特的内容。

en: At the same time AI can also be used to generate content for social media posts and chat sites. (18 words)
cn: 同时，人工智能也可以用来生成社交媒体和聊天网站的内容。

en: The main disadvantage of using AI to respond to customers is that there are concerns about trusting personal interactions to machines, which could lead not only to the subsequent loss of interpersonal connections, but also to a decrease in marketing personnel. (39 words)
cn: 运用人工智能来回应客户的主要缺点在于，人们会对人机交互可靠性产生的担忧，把人际互动交给机器不仅会带来人际交流的缺失，也会造成对市场营销人员的削减。

en: (47) Some believe that AI is negatively impacting on the marketer's role by reducing creativity and removing jobs, but they are aware that it is a way of reducing costs and creating new information. (31 words)
cn: （47）有些人认为，人工智能通过减少创造力和削减工作岗位，正在对营销人员的作用产生负面影响，但是他们也意识到这是一种缩减成本和创造新信息的方式。

en: By allowing AI to develop content some brand marketers may find that they are losing control over the brand narrative. (19 words)
cn: 通过使用人工智能来开发广告内容，一些品牌的营销人员可能觉察到他们正逐步失去对品牌描述的操控力。

en: (48) Algorithms used to simulate human interactions are creating many of these concerns, especially as no-one is quite sure what the outcomes of using AI to interact with customers will be. (29 words)
cn: （48）用于模拟人际互动的计算程序引发了许多诸如此类的担忧，尤其是因为没有人能完全确定使用人工智能与客户进行互动将会产生什么结果。

en: For AI to be successful, data needs to be accessible, but the use of personal data is becoming more regulated and the automated sharing of data is becoming more difficult. (29 words)
cn: 要使人工智能发挥功用，就要能够得到并使用数据，但是目前对于使用个人数据信息的监管越来越严格，自动分享数据也越来越困难。

en: (49) If customers are not willing to share data, AI will be starved of essential information and will not be able to function effectively or employ machine learning to improve its marketing content and communication. (31 words)
cn: （49）如果顾客不愿意分享数据，人工智能将无法得到必要的信息，从而无法有效地施展功用，也无法使用机器学习来完善其营销内容和传播途径。

en: Therefore, unless customers are prepared to sign release agreements, the use of AI may become somewhat restricted in the future. (19 words)
cn: 因此，除非客户有意签署公开个人信息协议，否则，运用人工智能在未来可能会受到一定的限制。

en: Not only can AI help to create the marketing content, but it can also provide a non-intrusive way of delivering the content to the target customers. (25 words)
cn: 人工智能不仅有助于开发新的营销内容，而且还能以一种不带干扰的方式向目标客户推送营销信息。

en: Data can be gathered on where the customer can be engaged, such as location, devices used, website interactions, and sites visited, to display marketing messages in appropriate forms, including emails, social media posts, pop-up advertisements, and banners at an appropriate frequency. (38 words)
cn: 可以在诸如位置信息、设备运用、网站交互、站点访问等客户授权的方面来收集数据，以恰当的频率和形式推送营销信息，推送形式包括电子邮件、社交媒体推送、弹窗广告和横幅广告。

en: (50) The non-intrusive delivery of the marketing messages in a way that is sensitive to the needs of the target customer is one of the critical challenges to the digital marketer. (29 words)
cn: （50）敏锐察觉目标客户的需求，以这样的方式不带干扰地推送营销信息，是数字营销人员面临的关键性挑战之一。

en: "Elephants never forget"—or so they say—and that piece of folklore seems to have some foundation. (16 words)
cn: "大象永不忘记"——或者人们常这样说——这个民间传说似乎有一些根据。

en: The African savanna elephant, also known as the African bush elephant, is distributed across 37 African countries. (17 words)
cn: 非洲草原象，也叫非洲丛林象，分布在非洲的37个国家。

en: They move between a variety of habitats, including forests, grasslands, woodlands, wetlands and agricultural land. (16 words)
cn: 它们在各种栖息地之间移动，包括森林、草原、林地、湿地和农田。

en: (46) They sometimes travel more than sixty miles to find food or water, and are very good at working out where other elephants are—even when they are out of sight. (30 words)
cn: （46）这些大象为寻找食物或水源，有时会跋涉六十多英里；并且它们非常善于判断其他大象的位置——即使其他大象不在视线范围内。

en: Using tracking devices, researchers have shown that they have "remarkable spatial acuity." (11 words)
cn: 研究人员使用跟踪设备表明它们具有"卓越的空间敏锐性"。

en: When finding their way to waterholes, they headed off in exactly the right direction, on one occasion from a distance of roughly thirty miles. (23 words)
cn: 在寻找水坑的过程中，它们总是准确地朝着正确的方向前进，有一次是从大约三十英里的距离。

en: What is more, they almost always seem to choose the nearest waterhole. (12 words)
cn: 更重要的是，它们几乎总是选择最近的水坑。

en: (47) The researchers are convinced that the elephants always know precisely where they are in relation to all the resources they need, and can therefore take shortcuts, as well as following familiar routes. (31 words)
cn: （47）研究人员确信，相对于它们需要的所有资源，大象总能精确地知道自己在哪里，因此它们除了遵循熟悉的路线，还可以走捷径。

en: Although the cues used by African elephants for long-distance navigation are not yet understood, smell may well play a part. (19 words)
cn: 尽管尚未理解非洲大象用于远距离导航的线索，嗅觉可能起到一定作用。

en: Elephants are very choosy eaters, but until recently little was known about how they selected their food. (17 words)
cn: 大象选择食物非常挑剔，但直到最近，人们对它们如何选择食物也了解不多。

en: (48) One possibility was that they merely used their eyes and tried out the plants they found, but that would probably result in a lot of wasted time and energy, not least because their eyesight is actually not very good. (36 words)
cn: （48）其中一种可能性是，它们仅仅使用眼睛去找到植物，再一一尝试这些植物，但这可能会浪费大量时间，消耗大量的能量，尤其是考虑到它们的视力实际上并不好。

en: (49) The volatile chemicals produced by plants can be carried a long way, and they are very characteristic: Each plant or tree has its own particular odor signature. (26 words)
cn: （49）植物产生的挥发性化学物质可以传播很远，而且非常有特点：每种植物或树木都有自己独特的气味特征。

en: What is more, they can be detected even when they are not actually visible. (14 words)
cn: 而且，即使实际上看不见这些气味，但这些气味也能检测到。

en: New research suggests that smell is a crucial factor in guiding elephants—and probably other herbivores—to the best food resources. (20 words)
cn: 新的研究表明，嗅觉在引导大象，以及可能是其他食草动物，找到最佳食物资源方面起着至关重要的作用。

en: The researchers first established what kinds of plant the elephants preferred either to eat or avoid when foraging freely. (18 words)
cn: 研究人员首先确定了大象在自由觅食时喜欢或避免的植物种类。

en: They then set up a "food station" experiment, in which they gave the elephants a series of choices based only on smell. (21 words)
cn: 然后，他们做实验建立了一个"食物站"，在这个实验中，他们只根据气味给大象一系列选择。

en: (50) The experiment showed that elephants may well use smell to identify patches of trees that are good to eat, and secondly to assess the quality of the trees within each patch. (30 words)
cn: （50）实验表明，大象很可能使用嗅觉，先找到适合食用的林区，然后再逐一评估每个区域内树木的质量。

en: Free-ranging elephants presumably also use this information to locate their preferred food. (12 words)
cn: 自由活动的大象可能也利用这些信息来找到它们喜欢的食物。

en: Their well-developed hippocampal structures may enable elephants, like rats and people, to construct cognitive maps. (15 words)
cn: 它们发达的海马体结构可能使大象能够像老鼠和人类一样构建认知地图。

en: Innovation and research have relied on public participation in science for centuries. (11 words)
cn: 创新与研究已经依赖于公众参与科学已有几个世纪。

en: It was a musician who discovered the planet Uranus in the 18th century by making his own telescope with mirrors composed of copper and tin. (25 words)
cn: 在18世纪，发现天王星的人是一位音乐家。他通过自制一台镜面由铜和锡组成的望远镜，发现了天王星。

en: (46) Recent decades have seen science move into a convention where engagement in the subject can only be done through institutions such as a university. (24 words)
cn: （46）近几十年来，科学发展形成了一种惯例，在这样的惯例下，人们只有通过像大学这样的机构才能参与该学科研究。

en: Citizen science provides an opportunity for greater public engagement and the democratization of science. (14 words)
cn: 公民科学提供了一个更广泛公众参与的机会，推动科学的民主化。

en: In the information era, large data sets, small teams and financial restrictions have slowed scientific process. (17 words)
cn: 在信息时代，大规模的数据集、小规模的团队和财务限制减缓了科学进程。

en: (47) But by utilizing the natural curiosity of the general public it is possible to overcome many of these challenges by engaging non-scientists directly in the research process. (27 words)
cn: （47）但是，通过利用公众天然的好奇心，让非科学领域的人直接参与到研究过程中，我们就可以克服上述挑战。

en: Anyone can be a citizen scientist, regardless of age, nationality or academic experience. (13 words)
cn: 任何人都可以成为公民科学家，无论年龄、国籍或学术经验如何。

en: You don't even need any formal training, just an inquisitive mind and the enthusiasm to join one of the thousands of citizen science projects to generate new knowledge and the means to understand a genuine scientific outcome. (35 words)
cn: 你甚至不需要任何正式培训，只需要一颗好奇的心，以及加入成千上万的公民科学项目的热情，这样就能生成新的知识，理解真正的科学成果。

en: (48) Scientists have employed a variety of ways to engage the general public in their research, such as making data analysis into an online game or sample collection into a smartphone application. (30 words)
cn: （48）科学家已经采用了各种各样的方式，吸引公众参与他们的研究，比如把数据分析做成一款网络游戏，或者把样本收集开发成一个智能手机应用。

en: They've implored citizen scientists to help with bug counting and categorizing cancer cells, and even identifying distant galaxies. (17 words)
cn: 他们已经请求公民科学家帮助计算昆虫数量、分类癌细胞，甚至识别遥远的星系。

en: This form of accessible science means that great minds are able to join the race to create and develop projects with the potential to change the world. (26 words)
cn: 这种可接触的科学形式意味着伟大的头脑能够加入到创造和开发有潜力改变世界的项目的竞赛中。

en: A citizen science-based approach can extend the field of vision and include more ideas and different brains to problem-solve and create, making innovation faster and more effective. (27 words)
cn: 基于公民科学的方法可以扩展视野，融入更多的想法和不同的智慧来解决问题与创新，从而使创新更加快速和高效。

en: The rise of citizen science has grown alongside the rise of do-it-yourself biology laboratories. (14 words)
cn: 伴随着公民科学蓬勃发展，自助生物学实验室也越来越多。

en: (49) These groups of people are part of a rapidly expanding biotechnological social movement of citizen scientists and professional scientists seeking to take discovery out of institutions and put it into the hands of anyone with the enthusiasm. (35 words)
cn: （49）公民科学家和专业科研人员的生物科技社会运动队伍在快速壮大，这群人就是他们中的一份子，他们试图将科学发现带出研究机构，并将其交到热衷于此的任意一个人手中。

en: There are around 40 official do-it-yourself biology centers across the globe in locations including Paris, London, Sydney, and Tel Aviv. (20 words)
cn: 全球大约有40个官方自助生物学中心，分布在包括巴黎、伦敦、悉尼和特拉维夫等地。

en: (50) They pool resources, collaborate, think outside the box, and find solutions and ways around obstacles to explore science for the sake of science without the traditional boundaries of working inside a formal setting. (33 words)
cn: （50）这些中心共享资源、互相合作、打破常规，克服障碍从而找到解决问题的途径和方法，旨在为科学而探索科学，而不受限于传统的正式工作环境。

en: So is it time to take the Petri dish out of the laboratory and into the garage? (17 words)
cn: 所以，是时候将培养皿从实验室拿到车库中去了吗？

en: Science education today revolves around the idea of scientific literacy—the base-level knowledge about science that nonscientists require to effectively get on in the world. (24 words)
cn: 当今的科学教育围绕着"科学素养"这一理念展开——即非科学专业人士在现实世界中有效生活与发展所必需的、关于科学的基础性知识。

en: This concept has served as a central goal for curriculum developers, local school boards, business and community leaders, and policymakers ever since its introduction nearly 80 years ago. (27 words)
cn: 自近80年前这一概念被提出以来，它一直是课程开发者、地方教育委员会、工商界和社区领袖以及政策制定者共同追求的核心目标。

en: (46) Tracing the history of the term, we can see how the definition of scientific literacy has shifted over time, muddying the waters when it comes to determining the goals of science education. (32 words)
cn: （46）追溯该术语的历史，我们可以看到"科学素养"的定义是如何随时间演变的，而这在确定科学教育的目标时把水给搅浑了。

en: And that's a shame, because there is much to recommend in the idea of scientific literacy as it was originally articulated in 1945, a time when science appeared to be the key to progress and scientists seemingly held the fate of the world in their hands. (43 words)
cn: 而这实在令人遗憾，因为"科学素养"这一概念在1945年最初被提出时，本身就有许多值得肯定之处——那是一个人们普遍认为科学是推动进步关键力量的时代，科学家似乎掌握着世界命运的走向。

en: (47) A return to that version of scientific literacy, which focused more on teaching what science is and how it works and less on memorizing scientific facts, seems like something society today desperately needs. (33 words)
cn: （47）回归那个版本的科学素养似乎正是当今社会所迫切需要的，因为它更多侧重于教授科学的本质及其运作方式，而非强调让学生死记硬背科学事实。

en: In the United States, the desire to provide the public with a general, nontechnical education in science originated as far back as the late 1800s. (24 words)
cn: 在美国，向公众提供一种普及性、非技术性的科学教育的愿望，早在19世纪末便已出现。

en: (48) Educators advanced the idea of having students complete detailed laboratory exercises in high schools in the belief that such work was beneficial primarily as a way to enhance logical reasoning and observational skills. (32 words)
cn: （48）教育工作者提出的观点是，让学生在高中完成详尽的实验室练习，他们坚信这类练习的主要益处在于增强学生的逻辑推理和观察能力。

en: The development in 1915 of the popular new subject "general science" was another effort to train students to apply the principles of science to everyday, nonscience problems. (27 words)
cn: 1915年，广受欢迎的新学科"普通科学"的出现，也是培养学生将科学原理应用于日常、非科学问题的又一次尝试。

en: Although these efforts were aimed at the nonscience-bound student, they never really made their way into mainstream thought and public discourse as a means to rally widespread support for the importance of science teaching in schools. (35 words)
cn: 尽管这些努力主要面向那些并不以科学为职业发展方向的学生，但它们始终未能真正进入主流观念和公共话语之中，因而也未能作为一种手段，动员社会对学校科学教育重要性的广泛支持。

en: (49) It wasn't until the phrase "scientific literacy" came along in the 1940s that science had the formidable slogan it needed to command public attention and make improving science education an important national goal. (32 words)
cn: （49）直到20世纪40年代"科学素养"这一术语的出现，科学才拥有了其所需要的强有力的口号，不仅赢得了公众的关注，还把增强科学教育确立为一项国家重大目标。

en: (50) The intense focus on scientific literacy in the United States originally grew out of the critical role of science and technology during World War II, as well as the perceived deficiencies of American soldiers. (32 words)
