cn: （50）美国对科学素养的高度关注，最初源于科学技术在第二次世界大战期间所发挥的关键作用，同时也因为人们察觉到美国士兵在科学素养方面的缺陷。

en: As the war unfolded, science rapidly assumed a central role. (10 words)
cn: 随着战争的推进，科学迅速占据了核心地位。

en: Battles increasingly depended on new military technologies such as radar and the proximity fuze. (13 words)
cn: 战斗越来越依赖雷达、近炸引信等新型军事技术。

en: Science-based analytical approaches proved remarkably successful in the hunt for German submarines in the Atlantic Ocean. (16 words)
cn: 以科学为基础的分析方法在大西洋搜寻德国潜艇的行动中取得了非凡成效。

en: And there was the (then-secret) work building the world's first atomic bomb. (11 words)
cn: 此外，还有那项当时仍属高度机密、旨在建造世界上第一枚原子弹的研究工作。

en: As a result, scientists—physicists in particular—found themselves in high demand. (12 words)
cn: 正因如此，科学家——尤其是物理学家——一时间成为炙手可热的人才。

en: "Sustainability" has become a popular word these days, but to Ted Ning, the concept will always have personal meaning. (20 words)
cn: 最近，“承受力”成了一个流行词，但对特德·宁来说，他对其含义有自己亲身的体会。

en: Having endured a painful period of unsustainability in his own life made it clear to him that sustainability-oriented values must be expressed through everyday action and choice. (27 words)
cn: 在经历了一段无法承受的痛苦生活后，他清楚地认识到，旨在提高承受力的价值观只有通过每日的行为和选择才能得到体现。

en: Ning recalls spending a confusing year in the late 1990s selling insurance. (12 words)
cn: 宁回忆起九十年代后期销售保险度过的那困惑的一年。

en: He'd been through the dot-com boom and burst and, desperate for a job, signed on with a Boulder agency. (20 words)
cn: 在经历了网络泡沫的膨胀和破灭后，他急需找到一份工作，因此就与博尔德公司签了约。

en: It didn't go well. (4 words)
cn: 但情况并不顺利。

en: "It was a really bad move because that's not my passion," says Ning, whose dilemma about the job translated, predictably, into a lack of sales. (27 words)
cn: “这的确是糟糕的一步，因为它激不起我的工作热情，”宁说。不出所料，工作上的进退维谷造成销售业绩不佳。

en: "I was miserable. (3 words)
cn: “我很痛苦，

en: I had so much anxiety that I would wake up in the middle of the night and stare at the ceiling. (20 words)
cn: 愁肠百结，常常在半夜惊醒，望着天花板发愣。

en: I had no money and needed the job. (8 words)
cn: 我身无分文，需要这份工作。

en: Everyone said, 'Just wait, you'll turn the corner; give it some time.'" (13 words)
cn: 大家都说，‘等等看，过一段时间情况会好转的。’”

en: Who would have thought that, globally, the IT industry produces about the same volume of greenhouse gases as the world's airlines do—roughly 2 percent of all CO2 emissions? (29 words)
cn: 谁会想到信息技术行业产生的温室气体总量会与航空业不相上下，约占全球二氧化碳排放量的2%？

en: Many everyday tasks take a surprising toll on the environment. (9 words)
cn: 信息技术行业的许多日常工作对环境造成了意想不到的危害。

en: A Google search can leak between 0.2 and 7.0 grams of CO2, depending on how many attempts are needed to get the "right" answer. (25 words)
cn: 每用谷歌搜索一次就会释放出0.2克至7.0克的二氧化碳，释放量的多少取决于使用者需要搜索多少次才能得到“正确”答案。

en: To deliver results to its users quickly, then, Google has to maintain vast data centres around the world, packed with powerful computers. (22 words)
cn: 为了把搜索结果迅速传输给用户，谷歌不得不在全世界范围内建立大型数据中心，并配备大功率计算机。

en: While producing large quantities of CO2, these computers emit a great deal of heat, so the centres need to be well air-conditioned, which uses even more energy. (27 words)
cn: 除了排放大量二氧化碳，这些计算机还释放许多热量，因此数据中心还需要良好的空调环境，而这又会消耗更多的能量。

en: However, Google and other big tech providers monitor their efficiency closely and make improvements. (13 words)
cn: 不过，谷歌和其他大型技术供应商已在密切监控其数据中心的工作效率并做出改进。

en: Monitoring is the first step on the road to reduction, but there is much more to be done, and not just by big companies. (24 words)
cn: 监控只是减排的第一步，需要做的还有很多，而且这不单单是大公司的事情。

en: When people in developing countries worry about migration, they are usually concerned at the prospect of their best and brightest departure to Silicon Valley or to hospitals and universities in the developed world. (32 words)
cn: 发展中国家的人担心移民问题时，通常忧虑的是自己国家最优秀最聪明的人都离开了，到硅谷或发达国家的医院和大学里工作。

en: These are the kind of workers that countries like Britain, Canada and Australia try to attract by using immigration rules that privilege college graduates. (24 words)
cn: 这些人正是英国、加拿大和澳大利亚等国家想要通过对大学毕业生提供优惠的移民条例来吸引的人才。

en: Lots of studies have found that well-educated people from developing countries are particularly likely to emigrate. (16 words)
cn: 许多研究表明，发展中国家受过良好教育的人尤其可能移民。

en: A big survey of Indian households carried out in 2004 found that nearly 40% of emigrants had more than a high-school education, compared with around 3.3% of all Indians over the age of 25. (35 words)
cn: 2004年对印度家庭的一项大规模调查表明，将近40%移居国外的人受过高中以上教育，而与之形成对比的是：全印度25岁以上受过高中以上教育的人约为3.3%。

en: This "brain drain" has long bothered policymakers in poor countries. (11 words)
cn: 这种“人才流失”现象长期困扰着贫困国家的决策者。

en: They fear that it hurts their economies, depriving them of much-needed skilled workers who could have taught at their universities, worked in their hospitals and come up with clever new products for their factories to make. (34 words)
cn: 他们担心这会损害本国经济，使他们失去急需的熟练劳动者，这些人本可以留在国内在大学任教、在医院工作或设计出新颖的产品供工厂生产。

en: I can pick a date from the past 53 years and know instantly where I was, what happened in the news and even the day of the week. (27 words)
cn: 我能从过去的53年中挑个日子，然后很快反应出自己在哪儿，当天有什么新闻，甚至是星期几。

en: I've been able to do this since I was four. (10 words)
cn: 从四岁起，我就有了这种能力。

en: I never feel overwhelmed with the amount of information my brain absorbs. (13 words)
cn: 我从来不会因为自己大脑吸收的信息量太庞大而感到难以承受。

en: My mind seems to be able to cope and the information is stored away neatly. (16 words)
cn: 我的大脑似乎有能力应对，而信息的储存也有条有理。

en: When I think of a sad memory, I do what everybody does—try to put it to one side. (18 words)
cn: 我想起伤心往事的时候，也和大家一样——尽量搁到一边不管。

en: I don't think it's harder for me just because my memory is clearer. (13 words)
cn: 我不认为记得越清就越难忘记。

en: Powerful memory doesn't make my emotions any more acute or vivid. (11 words)
cn: 惊人的记忆力并没有使我的情绪变得更为敏感或者更加细腻。

en: I can recall the day my grandfather died and the sadness I felt when we went to the hospital the day before. (22 words)
cn: 我能够记得祖父去世那天的情景以及前一天我们去医院时我有多么难过。

en: I also remember that the musical play Hair opened on Broadway on the same day—they both just pop into my mind in the same way. (25 words)
cn: 我也记得音乐剧《毛发》是在这天首登百老汇的——这两件事情就这样以相同的方式迅速跳进我的脑海。

en: Most people would define optimism as being endlessly happy, with a glass that's perpetually half full. (17 words)
cn: 大多数人将乐观定义为永远快乐，总觉得杯子里的水还有一半。

en: But that's exactly the kind of false cheerfulness that positive psychologists wouldn't recommend. (13 words)
cn: 但积极心理学家并不提倡这种虚假的快乐。

en: "Healthy optimism means being in touch with reality," says Tal Ben-Shahar, a Harvard professor. (15 words)
cn: “健康的乐观是与现实联系在一起的，”哈佛大学教授泰·本-沙哈说道。

en: According to Ben-Shahar, realistic optimists are those who make the best of things that happen, but not those who believe everything happens for the best. (25 words)
cn: 根据他的观点，现实的乐观主义者是去积极实现事情的圆满，而不是坐等事情会自己圆满。

en: Ben-Shahar uses three optimistic exercises. (5 words)
cn: 本-沙哈提出了乐观训练的三个阶段。

en: When he feels down—say, after giving a bad lecture—he grants himself permission to be human. (17 words)
cn: 当他心情低落时——比如，一个糟糕的演讲之后——他宽慰自己这是人之常情。

en: He reminds himself that not every lecture can be a Nobel winner, some will be less effective than others. (19 words)
cn: 他提醒自己不是每一次演讲都要求诺贝尔标准，有些演讲的效果会不如其他。

en: Next is reconstruction. (3 words)
cn: 下一个阶段是重塑。

en: He analyzes the weak lecture, learning lessons for the future about what works and what doesn't. (17 words)
cn: 他会分析这次失败的演讲，哪些地方可取，哪些不可取，为将来的演讲积累经验。

en: Finally, there is perspective, which involves acknowledging that in the grand scheme of life, one lecture really doesn't matter. (19 words)
cn: 最后一个阶段是前瞻，我们要认识到在生命的宏伟蓝图中，一次演讲根本算不上什么。

en: Think about driving a route that's very familiar. (8 words)
cn: 设想一下，你正开车行驶在一条非常熟悉的路线上。

en: It could be your commute to work, a trip into town or the way home. (15 words)
cn: 可以是你每天上下班、去城里、或者回家的路。

en: Whichever it is, you know every twist and turn like the back of your hand. (16 words)
cn: 不管是哪条路，你对每一个拐每一个弯都了如指掌，非常熟悉。

en: On these sorts of trips it's easy to lose concentration on the driving and pay little attention to the passing scenery. (22 words)
cn: 在这样的路途中，我们容易在开车的时候心不在焉，对途中的景色也几乎是全然不顾。

en: The consequence is that you perceive that the trip has taken less time than it actually has. (17 words)
cn: 结果是你觉得路途上所花的时间比实际要少。

en: This is the well-travelled road effect: people tend to underestimate the time it takes to travel a familiar route. (20 words)
cn: 这就是所谓的“熟悉路线效应”：人们往往会低估在熟悉的线路上所花费的时间。

en: The effect is caused by the way we allocate our attention. (11 words)
cn: 这种效应是由于我们注意力分配的方式不同引起的。

en: When we travel down a well-known route, because we don't have to concentrate much, time seems to flow more quickly. (21 words)
cn: 如果我们行驶在熟悉的路线上，因为不需要太集中精力，会感觉时间流逝较快。

en: And afterwards, when we come to think back on it, we can't remember the journey well because we didn't pay much attention to it. (25 words)
cn: 以后，一旦回想这段路程，因为注意力没有集中在此，我们的记忆就不那么清晰。

en: So we assume it was shorter. (6 words)
cn: 这样，我们就会觉得路程更短。

en: The supermarket is designed to lure customers into spending as much time as possible within its doors. (18 words)
cn: 超市旨在吸引顾客在店里停留尽量长的时间。

en: The reason for this is simple: The longer you stay in the store, the more stuff you'll see, and the more stuff you see, the more you'll buy. (28 words)
cn: 其原因非常简单：顾客在店里停留的时间越长，看到的商品越多；看到的商品越多，买的越多。

en: And supermarkets contain a lot of stuff. (7 words)
cn: 超市陈列了大量的商品。

en: The average supermarket, according to the Food Marketing Institute, carries some 44,000 different items, and many carry tens of thousands more. (21 words)
cn: 根据食品推广协会的调查，一般超市里有大约44000种不同的商品；大多数超市多达数万种。

en: The sheer volume of available choice is enough to send shoppers into a state of information overload. (18 words)
cn: 如此众多的选择足以使顾客面对的信息过于繁杂。

en: According to brain-scan experiments, the demands of so much decision-making quickly become too much for us. (17 words)
cn: 脑部扫描实验发现，需要快速做这么多的决定就会使我们太累。

en: After about 40 minutes of shopping, most people stop struggling to be rationally selective, and instead began shopping emotionally—which is the point at which we accumulate the 50 percent of stuff in our cart that we never intended buying. (39 words)
cn: 大约购物40分钟以后，大多人就不再努力做出理性选择，转而就开始冲动购物——此时，在购物车里我们已经装了一半根本没想买的东西。

en: My dream has always been to work somewhere in an area between fashion and publishing. (16 words)
cn: 一直以来，我梦想的工作是介乎于时尚与出版之间。

en: Two years before graduating from secondary school, I took a sewing and design course thinking that I would move on to a fashion design course. (26 words)
cn: 中学毕业前两年，我学习了缝纫设计课程，当时以为自己进而能够学习时尚设计。

en: However, during that course I realised I was not good enough in this area to compete with other creative personalities in the future, so I decided that it was not the right path for me. (35 words)
cn: 然而，就在这个课程上，我发现自己在该领域不够优秀，将来无法和其他那些有创造力的人竞争。因此我断定这条路不适合我。

en: Before applying for university I told everyone that I would study journalism, because writing was, and still is, one of my favourite activities. (24 words)
cn: 申请大学之前，我告诉大家我会学新闻，因为写作曾经是，现在依然是，我最喜欢的事情之一。

en: But, to be honest, I said it, because I thought that fashion and me together was just a dream — I knew that no one could imagine me in the fashion industry at all! (33 words)
cn: 但说实话，我之所以那样说，是因为我以为时尚于我而言只不过是一个梦想——我知道完全没有人会料想我会进入时尚行业！

en: So I decided to look for some fashion-related courses that included writing. (12 words)
cn: 因此，我决定去寻找一些既涉及写作又与时尚相关的课程。

en: This is when I notice the course "Fashion Media & Promotion". (10 words)
cn: 就在这时，我注意到了时尚媒体与营销这门课程。

en: A fifth grader gets a homework assignment to select his future career path from a list of occupations. (19 words)
cn: 有一个五年级的学生，收到了一门家庭作业，要他在一系列职业名单中选出自己未来的职业之路。

en: He ticks "astronaut" but quickly adds "scientist" to the list and selects it as well. (16 words)
cn: 他选了“宇航员”，但是又迅速在名单上加了个“科学家”，也选上了。

en: The boy is convinced that if he reads enough, he can explore as many career paths as he likes. (20 words)
cn: 这个男孩相信，如果读书够多，他就能随心所欲，走向各种不同的职业道路也行。

en: And so he reads—everything from encyclopedias to science fiction novels. (11 words)
cn: 因此，他博览群书，无论是百科全书还是科幻小说。

en: He reads so passionately that his parents have to institute a "no reading policy" at the dinner table. (18 words)
cn: 他痴迷于阅读，父母不得不规定他吃饭时，“禁止阅读”。

en: That boy was Bill Gates, and he hasn't stopped reading yet—not even after becoming one of the most successful people on the planet. (24 words)
cn: 这个男孩就是比尔·盖茨，他还没有停止阅读——哪怕已然是这个世界最成功的人之一。

en: Nowadays, his reading material has changed from science fiction and reference books: recently, he revealed that he reads at least 50 nonfiction books a year. (25 words)
cn: 如今，他阅读的材料不再是科幻类小说和工具类书籍：最近，他透露自己一年至少要读50本非小说类书籍。

en: Gates chooses nonfiction titles because they explain how the world works. (11 words)
cn: 盖茨选择非小说类，是因为这些书籍可以解释世界如何运转。

en: "Each book opens up new avenues of knowledge," Gates says. (10 words)
cn: “每一本书都会打开知识探索的新道路”，他说道。

en: It is easy to underestimate English writer James Herriot. (9 words)
cn: 人们很容易低估英国作家吉米·哈利。

en: He had such a pleasant, readable style that one might think that anyone could imitate it. (16 words)
cn: 他文笔风趣，可读性强，以至于有人觉得谁都能模仿。

en: How many times have I heard people say, "I could write a book. (12 words)
cn: 我曾无数次听人说：

en: I just haven't the time." (5 words)
cn: “我也能写书，只是没有时间而已。”

en: Easily said. (2 words)
cn: 易说，

en: Not so easily done. (4 words)
cn: 做到却难。

en: James Herriot, contrary to popular opinion, did not find it easy in his early days of, as he put it, "having a go at the writing game". (27 words)
cn: 和大家的看法不同，吉米·哈利自己说，在初期“从事创作活动”并不容易。

en: While he obviously had an abundance of natural talent, the final, polished work that he gave to the world was the result of years of practising, re-writing and reading. (29 words)
cn: 尽管他才华横溢，天赋异禀，但也经历了多年的写作实践、修订、审读，才最终为世人呈现出精雕细琢的作品。

en: Like the majority of authors, he had to suffer many disappointments and rejections along the way, but these made him all the more determined to succeed. (26 words)
cn: 跟大多数作家一样，他这一路走来，也遭遇到无数次的冷遇和失意，但这些都使他更加坚定，走向成功。

en: Everything he achieved in life was earned the hard way and his success in the literary field was no exception. (20 words)
cn: 他一生中所成就的一切都来之不易，在文学领域的建树亦非例外。

en: It's almost impossible to go through life without experiencing some kind of failure. (14 words)
cn: 人生，不经历点失败，几乎不可能。

en: But, the wonderful thing about failure is that it's entirely up to us to decide how to look at it. (21 words)
cn: 但，失败，奇妙之处就在于，决定如何看待它，完全取决于我们。

en: We can choose to see failure as "the end of the world". (12 words)
cn: 我们可以视其为“世界末日”；

en: Or, we can look at failure as the incredible learning experience that it often is. (16 words)
cn: 或者，我们也可以把失败看作一次难得的学习经历——它往往正是如此。

en: Every time we fail at something, we can choose to look for the lesson we're meant to learn. (19 words)
cn: 每每遭遇失败，我们都应该看到自己该汲取的教训。

en: These lessons are very important; they're how we grow, and how we keep from making that same mistake again. (20 words)
cn: 这些教训至关重要，它们让我们成长，避免我们重蹈覆辙。

en: Failures stop us only if we let them. (8 words)
cn: 只有当我们任由失败阻挡我们时，失败才会真正阻挡我们。

en: Failure can also teach us things about ourselves that we would never have learned otherwise. (16 words)
cn: 唯有失败，才能让我们认清自我。

en: For instance, failure can help you discover how strong a person you are. (13 words)
cn: 比如，失败能让我们发现自己有多么坚强。

en: Failing at something can help you discover your truest friends, or help you find unexpected motivation to succeed. (18 words)
cn: 遭遇某种失败，可以帮我们找到挚友，或者帮我们拾起自己都意想不到的强大动力，走向成功。

en: We tend to think that close friends and family members are our biggest sources of connection, laughter, and warmth. (19 words)
cn: 我们往往会认为，我们的人际关系、快乐和温暖，主要源自于朋友和家人。

en: While that may well be true, researchers have also recently found that interacting with strangers actually brings a boost in mood and feelings of belonging that we didn't expect. (29 words)
cn: 尽管这很可能是事实，但研究人员最近也发现，与陌生人交流，实际上能振奋情绪，增强归属感，这是我们未曾预料到的。

en: In one series of studies, researchers instructed Chicago-area commuters using public transportation to strike up a conversation with someone near them. (22 words)
cn: 在一系列研究中，研究人员指示芝加哥地区乘坐公共交通上下班的人与身旁的人互相攀谈。

en: On average, participants who followed this instruction felt better than those who had been told to stand or sit in silence. (22 words)
cn: 总的来说，参与者里面，按照指示做的人比那些被告知安静地站着或坐着的人感觉更好。

en: The researchers also argued that when we shy away from casual interactions with strangers, it is often due to a misplaced anxiety that they might not want to talk to us. (30 words)
cn: 研究人员还认为，如果我们羞于与陌生人随意闲聊，通常是因为我们会有不该有的担心，以为他们可能不想和我们说话。

en: Much of the time, however, this belief is false. (9 words)
cn: 然而，大多数时候，这种想法是错误的。

en: As it turns out, many people are actually perfectly willing to talk—and may even be flattered to receive your attention. (20 words)
cn: 研究结果表明，事实上，很多人非常愿意交谈——他们甚至可能会因为得到你的关注感到受宠若惊。

en: Although we try our best, sometimes our paintings rarely turn out as originally planned. (14 words)
cn: 虽然我们竭尽全力，但有时候我们的画作很少能表现出最初所计划的那样。

en: Changes in the light, the limitations of your painting materials, and the lack of experience and technique mean that what you start out trying to achieve may not come to life the way that you expected. (35 words)
cn: 光线的变化，绘画材料的局限，经验技巧的缺乏，都意味着你开始想要达到的效果可能不会如预计的那般栩栩如生。

en: Although this can be frustrating and disappointing, it turns out that this can actually be good for you. (18 words)
cn: 尽管这可能有点令人沮丧失望，但结果证明这实际上对你大有益处。

en: Unexpected results have two benefits: you pretty quickly learn to deal with disappointment, and realise that when one door closes, another opens. (22 words)
cn: 意料之外的结果有两大好处：你会很快就知道去克服失望情绪，且认识到一扇门关了，另一扇会打开；

en: You also quickly learn to adapt and come up with creative solutions to the problems the painting presents, and thinking outside the box will become your second nature. (28 words)
cn: 你也会知道调节，并且拿出富有创意的方案去解决绘画中出现的种种问题，创新思维会成为你的第二天性。

en: In fact, creative problem-solving skills are incredibly useful in daily life, with which you're more likely to be able to find a solution when a problem arises. (27 words)
cn: 事实上，创造性解决问题的能力在日常生活中相当有用，具备这样一些能力，一旦问题出现，你就更可能找到解决方案。

en: In the late 18th century, William Wordsworth became famous for his poems about nature. (15 words)
cn: 18世纪晚期，威廉·华兹华斯因其关于自然的诗作声名鹊起。

en: And he was one of the founders of a movement called Romanticism, which celebrated the wonders of the natural world. (20 words)
cn: 浪漫主义运动歌颂大自然的神奇，他便是这场运动的发起人之一。

en: Poetry is powerful. (3 words)
cn: 诗歌是有力量的。

en: Its energy and rhythm can capture a reader, transport them to another world and make them see things differently. (20 words)
cn: 诗歌的气韵可以抓住读者，将其置身于另一个世界，使他们能以不同的方式看待事物。

en: Through carefully selected words and phrases, poems can be dramatic, funny, beautiful, moving and inspiring. (15 words)
cn: 通过精雕细琢的字句，诗歌能扣人心弦、诙谐有趣，优美动人、鼓舞人心。

en: No one knows for sure when poetry began but it has been around for thousands of years, even before people could write. (23 words)
cn: 没有人确切地知道诗歌兴起于何时，但它已存在了数千年之久，甚至在人类能够书写之前就存在了。

en: It was a way to tell stories and pass down history. (11 words)
cn: 诗歌是一种讲述故事和传承历史的方式。

en: It is closely related to song and even when written it is usually created to be performed out loud. (18 words)
cn: 诗与歌紧密相连，即使是书面形式写下来的诗歌，也常常是为了大声表演而创作的。

en: Poems really come to life when they are recited. (9 words)
cn: 我们朗诵诗歌的时候，诗歌才真正变得鲜活起来。

en: This can also help with understanding them too, because the rhythm and sounds of the words become clearer. (18 words)
cn: 这也有助于我们理解诗歌，因为其声音和韵律变得更加清楚明白了。

en: With the smell of coffee and fresh bread floating in the air, stalls bursting with colorful vegetables and tempting cheeses, and the buzz of friendly chats, farmers' markets are a feast for the senses. (33 words)
cn: 咖啡和新鲜面包的香气飘散在空中，五颜六色的蔬菜和诱人的奶酪摆满了货摊，亲切的交谈声不绝于耳，农贸市场简直就是一场感官的盛宴。

en: They also provide an opportunity to talk to the people responsible for growing or raising your food, support your local economy and pick up fresh seasonal produce — all at the same time. (30 words)
cn: 它还让人们有机会与负责种植或养殖食物的人直接交流，支持本地经济，并买到新鲜的时令农产品——所有这些都可同时进行。

en: Farmers' markets are usually weekly or monthly events, most often with outdoor stalls, which allow farmers or producers to sell their food directly to customers. (24 words)
cn: 农贸市场通常每周或每月举办一次，大多设有户外摊位，使农民或生产商能够直接将食品卖给顾客。

en: The size or regularity of markets can vary from season to season, depending on the area's agricultural calendar, and you're likely to find different produce on sale at different times of the year. (33 words)
cn: 市场的规模或举办的次数因季节而不同，取决于当地的农业日历。并且你可能会发现在一年的不同时间有各种不同的农产品出售。

en: By cutting out the middlemen, the farmers secure more profit for their produce. (13 words)
cn: 因为省去了中间商，农民确保了自己农产品有了更多的利润。

en: Shoppers also benefit from seeing exactly where — and to who — their money is going. (15 words)
cn: 买东西的人也得到了好处，因为能清晰地知道自己的钱去了哪，给了谁。

en: You know the moment—the conversation slows, then there's a pause. (11 words)
cn: 你知道那种时刻吧——谈话慢了，然后就停了。

en: It's awkward, and so awkward that some people will panic and say anything. (14 words)
cn: 这种情况很尴尬；尴尬到有些人会惊慌失措，什么话都说得出来。

en: Do we all find such silences so stressful? (8 words)
cn: 我们都觉得这种沉默让人如此紧张吗？

en: Researchers analysed the frequency and impact of gaps greater than 2 seconds during conversations, including an overview of previous studies which indicate that the fear of awkward silences can be so extreme that people avoid talking to strangers, even though doing so is likely to be an enjoyable experience. (46 words)
cn: 研究人员分析了谈话中超过2秒的停顿频率及其影响，其中也概述了一些先前的研究，这些研究表明：人们极其恐惧令人尴尬的停顿，以至于他们会避免与陌生人交谈，即使与陌生人交谈可能是一次愉快的体验。

en: During conversations with short gaps, people feel more connected to their conversation partners. (12 words)
cn: 谈话中短暂停顿，会让人感觉与对方交谈更融洽。

en: But such feelings of connection markedly dip when entering a long gap. (12 words)
cn: 一旦停顿变长，这种相谈甚欢的感觉会显著下降。

en: Long gaps between strangers are likely to be followed by a change in topic. (14 words)
cn: 陌生人之间的长时间停顿，往往在接下来就会转移话题。

en: But the opposite seems to be true for conversations between friends. (11 words)
cn: 然而，朋友之间的谈话似乎正好相反。

en: Long gaps there saw increased connection. (6 words)
cn: 长时间停顿，见证了彼此关系的增进。

en: Between friends, longer gaps seem to provide natural moments for reflection and expression. (12 words)
cn: 朋友之间，较长的停顿似乎提供了反思和表达的自然契机。

en: The influence of wearables on psychology refers to how the clothes we wear affect our thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. (20 words)
cn: 穿戴对心理的影响，是指我们穿着的衣服如何影响自身的思想、情感与行为。

en: Clothing is not just about covering our bodies; it plays a significant role in shaping our self-perception and interactions with others. (22 words)
cn: 服装并不只是关乎遮身蔽体，它在塑造自我认知以及人际交往方面，都发挥着重要作用。

en: One aspect of this influence is self-expression. (7 words)
cn: 这种影响的一个方面就是自我表达。

en: The clothes we choose can reflect our personality, mood, and identity. (11 words)
cn: 我们挑选的衣服能够反映出个性、心境与身份。

en: Whether we opt for bold, colorful outfits or prefer more understated styles, our dress choices convey messages about who we are and how we want to be perceived. (28 words)
cn: 不管我们是钟情于大胆鲜艳的穿搭，还是偏爱低调内敛的风格，着装选择都在对外传递信息：“我们是谁”，以及“我们希望他人如何看待自己”。

en: Additionally, clothing can impact our confidence levels. (7 words)
cn: 此外，服装还能提高我们的自信心。

en: When we wear clothes that make us feel comfortable and confident, it can positively affect our self-esteem and overall mood. (20 words)
cn: 身穿让自己舒适自信的衣服，会对自尊心和整体情绪状态产生积极影响。

en: Moreover, cultural and societal influences play a significant role in shaping our dress choices. (14 words)
cn: 不仅如此，文化与社会因素也在很大程度上左右我们的着装选择。

en: Different cultures have their norms and expectations regarding dress, which can influence the types of clothing people wear and the meanings attributed to them. (24 words)
cn: 不同文化对衣着有不同的准则与期许，这既影响人们着装类型，也决定了服装所承载的含义。
